# Thread: Sean's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1673
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean Waters — 2018-01-24T14:10:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233331>

Hello everyone,

My background:
24, from Birmingham, UK - living in London as a Quantity Surveyor (Cost Manager for construction).

I was diagnosed with crohn's in 2015, but I've beat it with Kruse's protocols. But since Sep 2017 I've got Chronic Fatigue, Lethargy, Extremely bad Eczema on arms, hands and legs (back of the joints).

I started the Leptin Reset in Sep 17, along with a deep CT protocol. I did CT before, but this time everyday and much colder. I got my energy back, but the toxin-dump was major... I had at least one day off work per week for 3 months, either with IBD symptoms or Fatigue. Good days were ok but bad days were really bad. My Eczema remained bad throughout. My arms and legs are nearly scarred with all the scratching.

I went to South India (Goa) for 25 days from xmas eve to 19th January. Wow. What a change that made.

In the sun, sunrise to sunset everyday, eating what I wanted and feeling amazing. Eczema disappeared, fatigue disappeared, no gastro symptoms the whole time. I felt happy and full of life, my dopamine and testosterone surged and I had energy to burn everyday.

I got home and within 6 days things have started to flip. Everything is going back to how it was. I've decided I'm going to start selling everything I own, and eventually move to Mexico or wherever will work for me! I don't really care what I do as long as I'm outdoors... **suggestions please!!** I will be completing a Personal Training qualification here in the next 6 weeks before I leave, so hopefully I can use that in Mexico?? Maybe teach/coach sport or fitness to kids or adults. If not, I'd volunteer if I had to - I don't care about the word "Career" anymore... I just want SUN, that much is clear for me now.

I will likely have to split with my girlfriend, and my company may chase me for £5000 as they paid for my university final year, but I don't care anymore... none of it is worth the price I will pay if I remain here.

I met a couple in India who had a son who moved to Mexico and worked as a sports coach in schools teaching football. Then in the wet season (June July Aug Sep) he went to the US and coached Football (Soccer) in Boston, then back to Mexico City for the Dry season... If I could do something like this, but work in Yucatan area and teach in south of USA I would get UV-B all year round... That's what I'm hoping for. I'm also going to check workaway.org

Best wishes for your move and well done ;-)

### Sean Waters — 2018-01-25T11:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233438>

What a difference a day makes...

So I've been fasting on a 23/1 routine since Monday, then eating a big ass salad at about 7pm... Halibut fried in lots of butter + a big ketogenic salad, with some raw oysters on the side, followed by some peanut butter and cream for dessert. Following that, I shower at 7 deg. Celsius (44 deg. F) for around 15 minutes, then sleep and then wake up and CT shower again before work. Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday I felt cold and wasn't getting warm... but last night, I was letting the water hit that fatty spot on the back of your neck and boom I felt a surge of something in my body and warmth generated without shivering. Soon the water felt mild all over my body. I got out, had sex, and went to sleep.

Today I woke up and hit the shower again and felt and even more powerful surge of warmth and energy... just like I used to feel back in Sep 16 after I came back from a month in Bali (when I felt very optimal). After the shower, my energy sky-rocketed. I felt like I could run up and down the stairs and then spar with Conor Mcgregor for 5 rounds. I've literally not felt this way for well over a year.

I believe it's the combination of the UV rays I've absorbed in India for a month, Ketosis kicking in after 3-4 days and my body starting to burn fat for fuel, and Cold Thermogenesis being the gas pedal and activating both the fat burning and the UV and turning me into a Ferrari.

I'm really, really, amazed and shocked this has happened. It has given me hope that the next few months, just like Sep 16 - Dec 16, might actually be OK. But, instead of making the mistake of not seeing the sun for another 18 months, I will book a trip to my parents place in Spain for April for 1 week. Then, I'm going again end of May for 1 week. And again in August for 12 days.

My only concern is that once the water in London warms up in spring, that I will lose this benefit of CT and my energy will crash and I'll start to get fatigue. But, hopefully the UV in April and May will keep me going. The Sun returns to decent levels in the UK by May/June, and then July and August is usually rain LOL, but I will have the Spain UV again for 12 days end of August.

By October, I will have saved around £7-8000 and I could get a 6 month visa for India and spend the whole winter in South Goa where I know I will feel amazing. Or, if I can keep going I will do another 1 month trip at Xmas and wait until June 2019 when I no longer owe my company any money and I can actually leave for good with no worry of debt.

This is only one day, so have to take it as a pinch of salt, but there is a light of hope!

### Sean Waters — 2018-01-29T15:09:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233731>

Ok so update time...

I have been frantically trying to sort out some kind of "living situation" in central america, mainly mexico. Or somewhere hot.

i met a couple in India who have a son in Guatemala working in a hostel, he has set me up to go out there and work there, and potentially become manager if it works out ok. Its not paid, but free accomm, food and drinks.

Im handing my notice in within the next few days, and seeing when is the earliest opportunity to go.

One big problem is my girlfriend, she is working here in her dream job making furniture and she can't leave and won't leave. We are both absolutely gutted about this, but she knows I need to go and is not trying to make me stay.

Hopefully we can work something out.

But, for me, I will be moving to a tropical beach location, near the equator for the foreseeable future. I have enough money for a flight, and that is all I need. I can't wait to be living in paradise ☀️☀️☀️☀️☀️☀️

Will keep this updated before I go!

### Sean Waters — 2018-01-30T20:12:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233825>

So just visited the Doctors, as I needed a Doctors note to cancel a gym membership I was using for the Sauna before xmas and also to present to my company and enable me a few weeks off sick so to set this move in motion.

I told her about symptoms of Crohn's, Eczema and Fatigue... I got told I needed to go back to the Gastroenterologist for the Crohn's and "she couldn't stress this enough", basically, she can't even fathom how much I need to be back on drugs.

For the Eczema (non EMF rash), she gave me Hydrocortisone cream 1%. The stuff I took when I was ten that never worked and made my skin horrible and hair grow too fast. After 15 years they still haven't got past that useless cream?

And for the Fatigue I need blood tests to identify. So we will never know what they would've told me that was.

Overall, what was most interesting was each issue was broken out into parts, and had a specific drug or next step for each issue, it is never looked at as a whole or really thought of properly.

She spent the whole time looking into her Desktop computer screen and could barely, barely afford me a smile or any gratuities.

It was like dealing with a Cyborg, a robot, or some Artificial Intelligence. I even mentioned I had been to India, where she is from, not even a fucking blink.

Anyway, I got my doctors note and I will be handing my notice in to work tomorrow. I'm really trying to remain cool, because my body is consumed with fear of making this Quantum Leap... but I recognise it, I know it, and can feel it... but it will not stop me. I must conquer this phone call to my Boss... I must quit... I must make this happen.

The state of the GPs in the UK is on another level... They will never try to see the thing from a 30.000 ft view.. I do understand that they are extremely busy and they might want to help but they are tied down by the system... if they go investigate and start going deep then they are stepping out if the line... There is a cost involved and they can be held responsible.
I visited mine before Xmas and i had 2 issues however the second was not addressed as i was said i should have booked a double appointment.

You have to remember 1 thing you are super young and you can do a lot. If you can stand on your feet and work then you don't have anything to be afraid of. I left Greece when i was 23 and on a whim because i wanted to live free.. I never regretted it. You will do alright.

### Sean Waters — 2018-01-31T12:00:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233865>

I leaped.... Handed my notice in.

Got a feeling a huge wave of happiness, relief and adrenaline.... Dopamine - do what scares you!!

My line manager told me that it's a great shame but said that I need to look after number one. He wants to arrange a leaving meal for me with the lads at the office which is very good of him.

Waiting for a call back from HR so I can inform them directly, but I am over the fear now. I just need to know when I can leave, so I can book my flight to Guatemala and start selling all my shit that I don't need to raise some extra cash. Hopefully I will find out what they want to do about these Tuition fees that I owe them... I hope they will write it off, as I won't have a job or income to pay it back as I will be volunteering in Guatemala... unless they want £10 a month over 50 years they aren't going to get anything. We shall see.

My girlfriend has already said, she wants to work for 6 months and get experience and learn her craft, then she is going to come and join me. It's a long time but not long enough to split us up I hope.

Got a feeling life is about to get a lot, lot better

### kris90 — 2018-02-01T17:11:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233910>

Well I'm thinking of the business idea sooner than later. I found out they started testing 5G in November 2017 in Ottawa which is where I work. I've been sick 3 times now since around that time. My office is starting to drain me. My wife is at home 24/7 because she's on maternity leave, sleeps no more than 4 hours (up a lot with my son) and hasn't caught a single cold/virus.

I need to be able to work out of my own home now, because it's the only environment that's optimal.

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-01T18:00:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233916>

I think you definitely wise to pull away from the office life - even with CT, deep as possible, everyday, and a strict diet - it will eventually overpower you.

Could you work from home permanently?

Sounds bad, but being actually diagnosed with Crohns disease has been the biggest blessing for me, because it enables me to use the fact I have disease to gain advantage. For one, I could probably use it to enable a work from home life. Still productive, you cost less to the office/ company, and they are able to gloat about how they are flexible with disabled workers. You can exaggerate a bit of injury to get yourself a big gain... and they don't realise what you are doing. Nothing wrong with it, but just making use of a problem.

### kris90 — 2018-02-01T19:15:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-233923>

Always gotta be one step ahead for my family.

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-20T13:46:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-235180>

Update

Booked my flight to Guatemala City for 6th March 2018..... 17 hours with 1 change in Atlanta, and then a bus ride and ferry to the hostel I will be working at on the coast.

The Tuition Fees that I owe to my company have been written off by HR, because they deemed it unethical and morally wrong to pursue for the 5 grand because of the manner of my exit - resigning due to ill health. So I do not owe anybody anything, which is great to know.

I have been learning Spanish on duolingo for 2 weeks, I'm at 40% fluency according to the app. I feel like I can comprehend basic Spanish, and write it. Speaking it is more difficult. But I am impressed at how quickly you can pick it up with 1 hour per day or practice!

Long term... my plan is just to be in the Sun, learn Spanish, and more languages if possible. Also, get some experience working and hopefully managing the Hostel. This will open up opportunities to work elsewhere, and maybe get paid a little to be Hostel/ Hotel manager somewhere in the Yucatan or Costa Rica. Me and my Mother have spoke about maybe buying a Eco/Low nnEMF retreat or building one from scratch. That would be really cool I think, I could control the environment for me and my family that way, and have a reasonable income but most importantly Health and Happiness... I could also get my girlfriend to design the place. But this is way off, a bit of a long term dream/ goal to aim for. For now, I don't care about money or ambitions or careers. I care about getting my deteriorated Gut, Brain and Skin back to full health.

In fact, in contrast to the above, I've learnt that in this road to Optimal, it is so so important to not have any ties. The less things you have holding you back the better. To be optimal, you need to be able to migrate and wander at will... Literally as a free as a bird. I think any form of Business or Property or Loans etc. is only going to be detrimental to a Mitochondriac Black Swan. The less money and structure you "need" the more free you are to be healthy and happy.

Everyone at work is asking me, "how are you going to earn money?", "what you are working for free?!", "what about your career?", "what are you going to DO?".

These colleagues have Rheumatoid Arthritis and Crohn's Disease. Auto-Immune diseases = low dopamine.

Earning money, having a career and "doing" things is the ideals and ambitions of a low dopamine mind that does not understand or value nature.

### kris90 — 2018-02-20T15:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-235183>

Awesome attitude man! I'm excited for you. Dream big, and work towards it slowly. That's what I've really started to realise.

I visualize a similar long-term goal: to potentially open a Wellness Resort somewhere abroad to give people both great vacations, but also offer my wellness programs. I'm just taking babysteps toward something like this.

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-20T18:04:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-235195>

Yeah I think so - but I think dreaming is a human condition. The same condition that has put me in this bullshit position in the first place.

The Lion does not share any of my problems. Neither do any of the tribes that still exist.

They get food and water, explore their environment, teach each other and the young, make things with their hands, they party, sing, do some dancing and then fuck each other. The more I think about it, that is all I really need out of this life...

### Sean Waters — 2018-02-27T12:53:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-235835>

Update

Had my last weekend with my friends and family at home, this weekend coming is my last in London with my partner... there has been a lot of tears, people can get really upset and this can make it really difficult to leave, especially if you have deep emotional ties.

I see each event, each time someone gets upset, as a trial of my commitment and will to make myself better. I have passed all so far, because I remind myself how they never cried when I was in severe abdominal pain, or when I feel depressed, or when I can't get up in the morning, or if my skin is so damaged that I am embarrassed to wear a t-shirt. So I keep focused on myself and how I need to do this for myself, and how staying here would ease their pain but continue my suffering.

I also keep seeing signs and phrases everywhere that say encouraging things... It is really strange.

Anyone moving alone (without wife/ family), be prepared!

On a positive note, I have gone back and re-read Time 24 about 3 times and the Topological Insulators + Hemoglobin + Water + Light story is really making sense. I feel like I've finally started to make sense of how Sunlight actually makes it's way from our surfaces to the mitochondria of the deeper tissues and organs and improves our Redox and Deuterium Depletes us. It is incredible stuff.

I've also started drinking Voss Water and Icelandic Glacial Water in my final 2 weeks in the UK... noticed Energy, Sleep and Cognitive improvements... My dreams are incredibly clear and vivid... I still don't think it would be enough to keep me in the UK, but it might be the difference for others if they have the money and they really don't want to move!

One Week to go til I fly to Guatemala - **cannot wait!!**

### Sean Waters — 2018-03-02T09:10:05+0200

### Sean Waters — 2018-03-13T19:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-237123>

So I have been in Livingston, Isabel, Guatemala for nearly 1 week...

Free bed and food at a party hostel seemed a good idea but no bueno.

Sun has healed up a lot of my rash, made me relax and have good thoughts.

However, I also have a sore throat and feel close to a upper GI tract flare up, so had to fast for 48 hours. I am also still really really fatigued.

Observation of the locals show me a similar symptom, lethargic slow apathetic people here, most quite overweight.

No beach here, just dirty polluted garbage filled sea water with dead fish floating. It is a shame because I solved the fatigue in Goa by getting into the ocean. I do not want to do that here.

There is a 'siete alteres' or seven pools, like a cenote I guess, about 1 hour away. Will visit today or tomorrow. But overall this place is a C, and I want an A.

I have picked up quickly how hostels work within a week, and have already been offered to be a 'partner' in a chocolate cacao selling vegan hostel nearby... Sharing profits and running a place for owners who want flexibility.

SO MY PLAN IS... Get my arse up to Playa DC and walk in the hostels and hotels and offer to volunteer/ potentially manage the place on a very basic salary plus share of profits. Ambitious but fuck it let's take some risks... Things will work out.

I need my A... Sun, cenotes and ocean.

If anyone has any rooms I can stay in while I'm looking for a place in playa del C, please let me know... Would save me a few bucks and greatly appreciated.

### Sean Waters — 2018-04-02T22:14:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-238549>

So another 2 weeks has passed...

Still in Isla Mujeres with Shah!

My skin that has been exposed to UV is so dark now and practically no rash. Inside of my palms, my fingers, still got a bad rash and wake up some nights really thirsty and and a huge urge to scratch. It's like histamine activation via dehydration. I am icing them every night without fail but some day it's not enough.

My gut is fine, but it was in London so no worries.

My cognitive and fatigue symptoms though are fluctuating SO MUCH. It's like one day on one day off. Really confusing.

Me and Shah have seen every sunrise, every sunset, for 19 days together. I get lots of daytime UV, we are eating good - but no I can't seem to master the fatigue here.

We were eating carbs, and I remember I have a pyruvate kinase gene deficiency... So, even though some days my energy has been optimal on these carbs, I thought it might be that!

So ive gone ketogenic. Out of the 5 days on that, I've had 2 of the best and most energetic days on this whole trip. But I get Heart Arrhythmia, like palpitations or racing heart. And if I eat too much, or fast too much, I can wake up the next day with severe fatigue. Like today.

Maybe I'm adapting to keto, but this is similar to what I face in London. I'm questioning the 'benefits' of this island... I never had this issue in Goa really, an I ate carbs the entire trip.

I'm also running out of money, so I'm thinking of heading back to the UK to get some more money for a few months. I'm really not overall keen on living an isolated life in the tropics, and STILL being unable to move or work some days due to fatigue.

It's been a huge reality check coming out here, and a lot to decipher. We really are in a dystopian nightmare!

Hopefully this is just an adapting process and the ketosis will be the real deal like yesterday, but everyday and not just every other day!

### Sean Waters — 2018-04-03T00:04:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-238556>

So I've done some reading on fatigue.. and in the process my energy has come back. I ate another keto meal and I feel good.

I've read everywhere... healing chronic Fatigue = DHA, Ketones, Water and Sunlight.

We have ate local fish or shrimp everyday, today ive ate 32 pieces of shrimp, but I don't think either have enough DHA for fatigue.

I think I need sashimi salmon and tuna, plus Oysters.

In Goa I ate a whole Red Snapper or Tuna every few days. Here the quality has been poorer and WAY more expensive.

I'm just adapting to Ketosis, so maybe I need to just rest and wait to adapt. When the ketones kick in I feel world's apart from fatigue.

We are drinking gallons of purified water, but I can't verify quality. Apparently the tap water here is cenotes DDW! So I'm gonna try some...

I have found that Fizzy Sparkling Water helps me greatly, especially with lime. Also just eating pure coconut oil. Both deuterium depleters...

Maybe Isla Mujeres has been zapping me with EMF too. The Easter holidays brought a lot of people, and I had severe fatigue on another island... Bali, Indonesia.

### Sean Waters — 2018-04-07T19:03:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-238935>

My last 3 posts were written in a real low state.

But today I write in a much higher state!!! I'm elated.... I've found, I think, the cause for my problems the last year.

Salt. Sodium. Simple as that.

I've upped it to 2 to 4 tsp sea salt a day, plus ketosis....

My fatigue has reduced entirely to nothing. I'm still keto adapting in week 1. So not thriving just yet, but feel closer than ever.

My incurable skin rash on my hands from EMF, has disappeared in 4 days. Was it the sun? Well after 4 weeks in Goa, my inner hand rash was still there, and after 3 weeks here, still there, and I increase sodium to 5g and it goes. Not only that, but the itching in the middle of night was still there, as I stopped eating salt @4pm with last meal... So I wake up everyday fatigued and itching, even last 4 days....

Last night, I dab salt at 9pm.

I wake at 4am, feeling like I can go for a run! No itching! I think the lack of sodium is allowing calcium efflux to run riot with the EMF from phones and WiFi in my mitochondria in my hands and skin. I get dehydrated by losing DDW and EZ water due to lack of salinity/ sodium. Then in response Histamine sky rockets and I wake up ready to literally bite my fingers off they itch that bad... But with the salt pre-sleep holding the calcium channels together and keeping the water salinity protected. It doesn't happen. Rash gone, in 3 days. Incredible. No amount of CT could achieve this! But salt can... Wow.

No lie in bed either. I had so much energy. Was at the sunrise beach 1 hour early, the moon was out. I haven't felt like this in nearly 2 years. Shah78 has never seen me up before 6am, I was up at 4am with energy!

I'm amazed.

Not only have I beat Crohn's, but now iv beat another fucker - chronic fatigue, EMF sensitivity and depression.

This trip has exposed me to high levels of Sunlight. And nature has revealed the answers to me in response to my increased dopamine and awareness level.

I do not doubt, I would still be totally unaware of the simple answers I've gained were it not for this UV 11 SUNLIGHT.

Sun + Ketosis + DHA + 4-6g sodium = energy and happiness for CFS / DEPRESSION, EMF SENSITIVITY AND CROHN'S.

I don't get any fatigue in the sun, if I do, dab the salt, instantly back in the game. Incredibly simple. It's all water chemistry and Jack has scattered info if you need more science. But it's simple once again. Simple natural principles. Thank you Jack!

If I'm honest, I have hated being isolated out here and I don't actually like Mexico as a place. So, I'm glad to be going home with hope that I can manage and overcome my problems and still be with my family and friends.

I'm gonna stay out another 3 weeks then head home to see my girl who is dying to see me, and all my family and friends.

I've learnt an incredible amount about life here. New perspectives on everything. I will be going home to go freelance, so I can take off when I need to.. and I will probably go to Goa, so cheap and I love it there. But hopefully 1 or 2 months a year will suffice for now, and not a permanent move away from everything you have back home!

I was willing to do it, for health, but I'm relieved I don't need to!

I highly recommend a month or 2 out here to anyone, Shah78 will take care of you and hopefully you can find your own answers.

Over and out

### Sean Waters — 2018-06-28T13:09:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-244490>

Chronic fatigue and depression have just vanished following a Leptin Rx and Grounding protocol with no meat or eggs or nuts due to a hypothesis that I have a serious 06 issue. I just Wild Scottish Salmon and Octopus and Seaweed everyday. I also eat Sauerkraut, onion + garlic raw and lots of ghee and coconut oil. Of course i use liberal Salt, between 3 and 5 teaspoons per day. Depending on Sun/blue light/nnEMF exposure.. increasing as they increase. I'm also taking 2-3 grams curcumin, 1-2g of Quercetin + Ester C (as i have serious hay fever without it), and I was taking 200mg Betain HCL pre-BAB but now stopped that and felt better - it was giving me Nausea/ reflux.. so ive optimised my stomach acid. Another big win! I have also stopped B vitamins as they did absolutely fuck all for me... note to anyone with high cysteine/low methionine and likely MTHFR - don't bother with supplements!!

I'm in my 8th week of the Leptin Reset - and my Chronic Fatigue is in my rear view mirror. Which is unbelievably fucking amazing to be rid of that bastard of a condition. I am back at work and earning money once again and life is going extremely well considering where I was 6 months ago. I'm extremely productive and efficient. My brain is working at top gear and I'm getting better and better everyday. My gut is also good but definitely could be better - although I've found i no longer need any HCL before BAB now which is amazing. There have been a few detox days but otherwise my gut is in the best condition possible.

Except for this Eczema..... which is symmetrically placed on my Palms and Fingers, and the back of knees, inside elbow areas and groin. Classic eczema basically. It wakes me up @ 4am before i rise @ 4.30/5am everyday for intense histamine reactions and my whole body feels HOT! I also cannot describe the pleasure and urge i get to scratch, it is irresistible... It's like i become possessed.

I hold 2 black tourmaline crystals everyday to stop my hands from flaring at work. I ground them essentially - and it has worked very well. I've also practiced Grounding everyday for 1 hour in AM with sunrise and 2 hours in PM with sunset. I got 11 hours grounded both days at the weekend in full UV index of 3 (9am), 5 (10am) to 8 (1pm) to 1 (7pm) and sunset at 9pm.

After a bad flare on 20th from what i perceive is lack of grounding (only factor that changes) after my biggest CT i'd ever done - 65 minutes @ 52.3 F, i figured CT wasn't helping as it should. The grounding stones and the grounding protocol has slowly healed me over the last week and it's in much better condition.

However, I want to banish this thing forever and finally be free of any "problems" so I can optimise to new levels.

I'm looking for ideal Labs to get for Eczema. JK recommended Brain Specific Nutrients and an O6/03 ratio in his early writings around 2012/2013 in various areas of the site and MDA thread.

I have a severe issue with O6's... whenever i eat them i crash or get a severe irritability reaction or Insulin/fatigue problem and a load of angry violent thoughts. I'm eating pure 03 at the moment, with only 06s coming from cocnut oil and ghee. I can't even tolerate olive oil.

My ratio test arrives soon to confirm my theory of a bad ratio. But it could be a lack of selenium too...

I am considering the Trace Metals/ Minerals test with Selenium. Are there any other tests for Eczema that i should consider?

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-05T19:17:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-245050>

I went back over some research on Histamine after ANOTHER FLARE UP OF ECZEMA on the early morning of 2nd july, my 25th, before i woke up.

I tore apart my hands again by rubbing them together. The bit inbetween middle finger and ring finger on my left hand was actually red raw, no skin left on it!

This was a severe histamine reaction. So I got to work and researched like no tomorrow - I found a lot of research on Simplified or Bad Gut bacteria releasing histamine. When this happens all day in response to your food and your environment, the histamine glass fills slowly and slowly until it spills over and you have a major attack. My hands get the brunt of this, but also back of legs and arms and groin are bad too.

I found that Berberine could be used as an anti-bacteriial/ fungal and had some studies done on blocking histamine. In the meantime, it would act like Metformin, but a natural version, and "reset my gut flora clocks" and also boost Cytochrome 1 ROS (I think) like ECCG does and other slight mito poisons, exercise does this too i believe. I decided to order it, and by chance I was able to get an evening delivery on my birthday.

I got home and popped 1.6grams of it: Berberine sulphate 400mg, citrus seed extract 400mg, Artemisin 60mg, Black Walnut Hulls 400mg x 4 tablets.

Within an hour, I felt euphoric and a slight altered state of consciousness. My whole body relaxed and I felt much more open to contact with my missus. I sat and grounded in the sun in the park and just felt more and more amazing. My rash on my hands stung intermittently, but then quickly dried and felt a lot better.

Over the next few days my rash got better and better. 3 days later on 5th July and my rash on my hand is now 70-80% clear and hasn't itched in days (miracle). The other areas of my body are healing but at a slower rate, and with much less itching.

I've been clutching 2 pieces of Black Tourmaline crystal the last few weeks and this week my Hand's rash has leaked slimey sticky water all over them, especially in the morning! Is that Deuterium? Or just white blood cells? God knows. Whatever it is, I've noticed a correspondence in reduction of swelling - it's almost like the rash is passing the protons on to the negative rocks/ crystals. I think these rocks have helped speed up the recovery of my hands quicker than other areas as it has Grounded my hands and allowed this "leak" of protons quicker than elsewhere.

So, my Rash must be a Gut Flora issue (probably going back to my Crohn's disease days) and a Histamine clearance problem/ overload of histamine from bacteria.

**THERE IS MORE!!......**

Simultaneously, in the last couple of days, my body has gone through some very encouraging signs of Leptin Sensitivity being regained (I am on the reset on my 9th week as of Monday).

I have been eating a BAB of 450g Wild Scottish Salmon, 50-100g Seaweed (Wakame, Dulse, Kelp, Arame, Hijiki), 1/2 onion raw and 6-8 tbsp Ghee (UK readers - i have an awesome supplier of salmon, PM me)

At lunch i have followed with 300g Wild Salmon and Seaweed and Ghee

Dinner, only the last few days has been SMALLER than lunch - i misread the Reset guide and thought lunch was small and dinner medium but that is the POST RESET GUIDE. WHAT AN IDIOT.

So last few days, Dinner is 100-150g Salmon and 2-3 tbsp ghee and seaweed.

I take the Berberine mix with each meal, 3 at BAB, 2 at lunch and 3 at dinner.

Within 3 days of doing so - I released a huge amount of Heat after lunch and started sweating, my heart was racing too. According to Jack this is T3 optimisation and UCP1 and 3 activated. I had endorphins too.

Then this morning I had BAB and could barely finish my normal 450g Salmon, I just about got 350-400 down... so gone from 90g protein to 70 in a day.

Then I had endorphins on the way to work, and my sense of smell became acute! I smelt everyones coffee and all of the chocolate croissants and crepes and they gave me endorphins! BUT NO CRAVINGS! i just shut my thoughts off easily, closed my eyes and breathed in deeply and it elicited even more Endorphins. I felt unreal.

I get to work and the endorphins go (of course!!) but then I haven't fucking got hungry in the last 12 hours since my 5am BAB...

LITERALLY NO HUNGER.

My mouth has salivated a little, and belly tingling now @ 12 hours, but everytime it does there is just this big wave of fullness that cuts in. It's like the ghrelin and agouti are having a little battle.

I just finished Brain Gut 7, 8 and 9 too... which shows how Bacteria Flora can induce Leptin Resistance via Lipopolysaccharide release and affect SOCS3. I think this has been my issue. The Berberine has stepped in and smote them down (I've had a few trips to the bathroom this AM!), and it has also improved my insulin sensitivity too I think.

It has basically Turbo-boosted my Leptin Reset! Berberine for the fucking win!

Feeling very positive, and i'd just ordered £500 worth of labs to test for Leptin Resistance to see where I was. I don't know whether to take them now? Or whether to wait a few weeks until on the Post Reset diet and get a gauge of baseline health and where I can optimise. I think i'll do the latter. Too much change at the moment! Waste of £500.

Anyway, starting to finally feel hunger now, just a little.... Will keep updated!

But anyone with Fatigue, Depression, Eczema, Crohn's/ Auotimmunity..... PLEASE SEE THE ABOVE !! THE BASIC PROTOCOLS JACK GIVES US MIGHT BE THE ANSWER! DON'T RUSH TO MOVE TO MEXICO UNTIL YOU HAVE DONE THESE !!!!

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-10T16:02:03+0300

More updates......

Had a Seafood tapas meal with EVOO for first time since April and suffered a mild fatigue. I really think my 06/03 raqtio is going to be fucked - results back this week hopefully tomorrow.

At least it wasn't severe fatigue or a crash, which it used to be. This was mild and manageable. It has affected my hunger for a couple days too. Only today have i been able to go without lunch again.

Got a feeling I was heading toward diabetes before Mexico/ Leptin reset/ strict avoidance of 06 epi paleo

My friend (also on the reset) suggested an early bed time. Pre-sunset @ 8-9pm... I tried it, and it's the first time I've actually gone 2 whole nights without scratching anywhere on my body. The severity had reduced with the Salt and epi paleo, but would always be itching somewhere a little bit!

So bed at 8.30 for 9pm asleep, and i wake up at 4 now. I feel WAY more refreshed and less stressed, i feel refreshed on waking and not once stood up. My libido has increased too.

Feel close to end of the reset now - looking onwards to Post reset diet/ exercise. Considering barefoot sprints in park and some weights, but my mind says screw it and just CT/ Ground instead.

I think i'm gonna go another week on Leptin rx and cement this new pattern into my brain and then start adding some exercise to see my reaction n=1!

Will keep going and report back.

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-11T12:40:30+0300

Last 2 nights since i've started the extra early sleep protocol - firstly i've found it quite difficult to fall asleep before 9am, and then i've been waking @ 2.30/3am, then falling back to sleep til 5am.

I know the Growth hormone rtelease is 3-5 so i don't want to be interrupting that cycle at all!

However, I can't deny the fact that I feel GOOD @ 3am and @5am, and my eczema hasn't night itched AT ALL.

This could just be a little adaption to a different cycle, or it could be worse - Cortisol issues.

Reading EE9 Quantum Sleep - DC Current, Melanopsin (darkness at night) is key for the flow of CSF to induce sleep.

The remedy is either:
1\. go back to sleeping after sunset instead of before (but risk the eczema returning in sleep)
2\. Grounding before bed watch sunset to get more DC current
3\. Throw up the black out blind @8pm and start the darkness to the eye
4\. Start CTing pre-bed

I do #2 every night anyway. So I will try the others over the next week and see what happens.

STARTED THE LEPTIN POST RESET TODAY.....

Just felt less hungry at BAB and thought fuck it - let's see what happens... Felt absolutely amazing after breakfast (even despite the messed up sleep pattern), best energy i've had. Felt so light and loose, full of energy... moreso than after BAB lately. **That tells me it's the right move!**

For lunch i will have a small amount of fish and oil to keep me going then for Dinner i've got Wild Trout and Bass with Samphire and Ghee and coconut oil for a big 75g protein meal @ 4-5pm.

### drezy — 2018-07-11T21:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-245669>

Here is from my "random notes on some British guy" file:

“Berberine upregulates the LDL receptor (LDLR) by a mechanism distinct from that of the statins, which involves stabilising the LDLR mRNA”

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15926873](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15926873)

"The Series 2 prostaglandins play a role in swelling and inflammation at sites of damage or injury. They also play a role in inducing birth, in regulating temperature, lowering blood pressure, and in the regulation of platelet forming and clotting. The role of Series 2 Prostaglandins does serve a vital role for the body for without it you would bleed to death from the slightest cut. However, in excess (think high omega 6 content of the diet), these prostaglandins are harmful and many diseases are directly linked to excessive inflammation and blood clotting."
&
"The organic chemistry of the success of the ketogenic diet is based upon the following. Dietary fasting or the use of high dietary MCT’s (coconut oil), stimulates the beta oxidation (fat breakdown in mitochondria) of very long chain fatty acids that occurs in most neurodegenerative disorders and some disorders with developmental delay like autism. This action immediately depletes the brain of its lipid supply and causes the prostagladins to be unregulated. Moreover, the use of simultaneous dietary carbohydrate ingestion increases the release of insulin peripherally which depresses delta 6 de-saturase enzyme and stimulates the production inflammatory prostaglandin series two chemicals which destroy the brains stores of DHA and EPA and this slowly erodes the brain’s lipid content. This can be offset by increasing transcytosis at the astrocytes if there is enough dietary LDL made by the liver."

[https://jackkruse.com/why-the-epi-paleo-rx-is-stat-paging-your-brain/](https://jackkruse.com/why-the-epi-paleo-rx-is-stat-paging-your-brain/)

### Sean Waters — 2018-07-12T11:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-245711>

Hahaha i really appreciate the input mate - i can't do this on my own!!

I think you are saying that my ketogenic diet is breaking down fats all the time in my brain and this is causing lots of swelling/inflammation due to prostaglandins (mast cells for eczema, hay fever) and the Berberine has assisted in upregulating LDL and therefore increased transcytosis and offset that problem.

You are also saying i could have the FADS1 and 2 gene turned off which = high amount of eczema and low amount of 06s??? Correct me if i'm wrong!! Probably totally misinterpreted this.

If thats what youre saying ^ It is definitely a possibility. I think i need a 23andme test asap to rule this out........... I was born with a rash from birth. My father was on strong anti-psychotic drugs for a couple years pre-birth and during and after. Chlorpromazine was the main one, my mom told me.
[https://patient.info/medicine/chlorpromazine](https://patient.info/medicine/chlorpromazine)

I had real allergy problems since birth. Eczema appeared when I was 3 years old. Not breastfed either - got put on soy milk as dairy made me react. As i got older dairy was fine, so maybe i was just allergic to something else and they got it wrong. I think my 23andMe could tell me a lot about my birth and genetics.

HOWEVER - I have the genes for Crohn's, depression, fatigue etc. as i've had these things BUT i've also managed to BEAT these things into oblivion with a change of diet, light exposure, CT, grounding etc. Like, literally these things have vanished. Crohn's is irreversible according to modern medicine, but it has gone - for coming up to 3 years i've been on no medication. I was off them in 6 months actually. So this tells me that SNPs are good info but they don't matter. Which leads me to my next point............

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Another option could be that the Eczema is Gut Bacteria Dysbiosis = I had a stool analysis done by Genova in 2016 (I had been off crohn's meds for 3 months and felt better, but still eczema and "anger and irritability" as well as some fatigue.

My functional medicine doc found that there were massive signs of dysbiosis. I will upload the results when i find them. At the same time, i had eczema on the top of my hand, she actually put me on the Berberine product i'm using now along with a Zinc and Plant Tannin mix for 1 month and the rash almost disappeared. Then the next month was a Gastromend month of liquorice root and other stuff to heal me. Month 3 was probiotics and N-acetyl cysteine. She also put me on Methyl B vitamins for poor b vitamins and a suspected MTHFR snp due to low methionine/high cysteine and low B12, B6, B9, B2 etc. etc. They didn't seem to do ANYTHING for me.

I finished the programme but found ketosis and fasting and CT at that time and felt wonderful - i assumed my progress was down to that and not her supplements. But i think she was right with dysbiosis, just wrong on the SNP stuff.

I'm back on the Berberine product and i've noticed changes in my rash and in my bowel movements. I've only been taking them 10 days and within 1 week i've become leptin sensitive. I feel incredible and much less "taxed". My energy, mood and itching habits have all improved. Hay fever has disappeared.

Could it be that I had some nasty bugs producing histamine??!! and now they are getting killed off??

I don't know - because the rash clearance has STALLED the last few days, so i'm not sure anymore.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* I think i need more tests!

This morning i ate Fruit for the first time in months with my breakfast for this post-reset. I also ate Almond butter and Brazil nuts and felt fine!! I feel like i need to eat more 06 to level out that ratio. Maybe 06s aren't my issue after all. As Doc says, eliminating food is never wise.

### drezy — 2018-07-12T21:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-245764>

I wouldn't discourage you from a genetic test. I also wouldn't encourage you to SNP gawk/navel-gaze too much either.

I keep a little file on myself of previous tests and I throw notes in here and there. One note has "MTHF ?" written on it (with the question mark X'ed out now). Another note has "Diabetes ?" written on it. Not that I experience any symptoms, but my mom has it and long ago before all this I decided I'd slowly alter course and eat like a diabetic should little by little over a long "course correction". That started me down the whole paleo path and eventually here.

I just sent you the above because you mentioned Ω6 concerns/symptoms and berberine relief. I looked up berberine and saw the LDL connection then remembered a blog mentioning 6 de-saturase enzyme suppression and inflammatory prostaglandin overproduction.

I figured I'd just alert you to one possibility that either your prostaglandin D2 is getting overproduced OR your DP Receptors (mast cells aka itch/eczema) could be hair trigger.

I'm the last guy to overly identify with or kvetch about SNPS since I've been able to shut down MTHF symptoms I've had since I was 14. Some of the pattern matches the constellation of symptoms and successes you've had.

If you ever download a big old genetic analysis and would like to do a little looking for a needle in the haystack let me know. I could show you how to search through it and use some tools out there to start understanding your code. Obviously stick with what is working for you and what Doc recommends. I'm not a SNP gawker but it is a little fun to get lost a bit in data sometimes.

> maybe the Bear gets irritated and pissed off too when he starts eating fruit, and starts overreacting at his bear girlfriend for being on her iphone at night and stuff when he's trying to get some sleep.

I'm positive I saw David Attenborough narrate this in Planet Earth:
"Watch her mate closely as the female bear reaches out to check her instagram. Images of salmon, berries, and bears attempting to make as "duck" a face as bear's faces can "duck" fill her mobile screen as her partner grows visibly agitated."

When the trees get loaded I will experience physical agitation a little in my eyes about the same that dust would cause. It's nice to ditch allergies.

Jack responds, POMC makes the endogenous endocannabinoids as I laid out in my LinkedIn blog about the sun......in the 250-780nm light range but THC is optimized in 340-500nm for the plant. ### Sean Waters — 2018-07-20T17:17:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-246272>

**
[Dr. Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?hc_ref=ARQzvjt7qzv-AMWCbX_I5sHmF9mrIeoTsrbMA2HssyehQj97H-p3nX8cBC9otzYwxkI&fref=nf)**
[May 22](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/2147619441969065) ·
**Beta-endorphin is made via POMC and solar exposure of the eye.** Morphine is an exogenous opioid that is used for people in pain. People deficient in solar exposure in the IRA, UVA, and UVB ranges have more pain and require more opioid drugs than those who get outside more often. This implies morphine dosing is not quantized to the light we live under and may lead to collateral effects. How is this information transferred in tissues? By CSF water networks in the brain loaded with H+. Shocker to no mitochondriac. Morphine tolerance is mediated by complex formation between the vasopressin 1b receptor, β-arrestin-2, and the μ-opioid receptor.[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41593-018-0144-y](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fs41593-018-0144-y&h=AT2JcFUgtlh0Gk18vPJc8GZ50NViMVKmNc_3HVdoaHF5EOI3d3SXvOPi3leCVpSCsr8c5XYgtR44m5Ma6iZfljhOIBgfLL6N2_G95xWANcNzhzk9OL-k3gEdLk-HMOLikUwon2kmmhw-uqXWPEr2)

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/time-rethink-your-truth-sun-jack-kruse/](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/time-rethink-your-truth-sun-jack-kruse/)

UV-A has a big surprise function that is buried in this picture below: It increases a protein made in the brain called POMC. From this protein, many other proteins are cleaved by solar light to stimulate many important pathways, like dopamine and melatonin creation in the eye and skin. **Another important one is β-endorphin release. Sunlight releases an opiate when we are exposed to the sun.** Guess why nature did that? Could it be that she wanted us going in the sunlight often for its healing powers? I think so.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25707796](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25707796)

**Hypothalamic POMC neurons promote cannabinoid-induced feeding.**
Hypothalamic pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC) neurons promote satiety. Cannabinoid receptor 1 (CB1R) is critical for the central regulation of food intake. Here we test whether CB1R-controlled feeding in sated mice is paralleled by decreased activity of POMC neurons. We show that chemical promotion of CB1R activity increases feeding, and notably, CB1R activation also promotes neuronal activity of POMC cells. This paradoxical increase in POMC activity was crucial for CB1R-induced feeding, because designer-receptors-exclusively-activated-by-designer-drugs (DREADD)-mediated inhibition of POMC neurons diminishes, whereas DREADD-mediated activation of POMC neurons enhances CB1R-driven feeding. **The Pomc gene encodes both the anorexigenic peptide α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone, and the opioid peptide β-endorphin. CB1R activation selectively increases β-endorphin but not α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone release in the hypothalamus,** and systemic or hypothalamic administration of the opioid receptor antagonist naloxone blocks acute CB1R-induced feeding. These processes involve mitochondrial adaptations that, when blocked, abolish CB1R-induced cellular responses and feeding. Together, these results uncover a previously unsuspected role of POMC neurons in the promotion of feeding by cannabinoids.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2018708/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2018708/)

The precursor protein, proopiomelanocortin (POMC), produces many biologically active peptides via a series of enzymatic steps in a tissue-specific manner, yielding the melanocyte-stimulating hormones (MSHs), corticotrophin (ACTH) and β-endorphin. The MSHs and ACTH bind to the extracellular G-protein coupled melanocortin receptors (MCRs) of which there are five subtypes. The MC3R and MC4R show widespread expression in the central nervous system (CNS), whilst there is low level expression of MC1R and MC5R. In the CNS, cell bodies for POMC are mainly located in the arcuate nucleus of the hypothalamus and the nucleus tractus solitarius of the brainstem. Both of these areas have well defined functions relating to appetite and food intake. Mouse knockouts (ko) for _pomc_, _mc4r_ and _mc3r_ all show an obese phenotype, as do humans expressing mutations of _POMC_ and _MC4R_. Recently, human subjects with specific mutations in _β-MSH_ have been found to be obese too, as have mice with engineered β-endorphin deficiency. The CNS POMC system has other functions, including regulation of sexual behaviour, lactation, the reproductive cycle and possibly central cardiovascular control

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Jeong and colleagues test the hypothesis that POMC neuronal activity increases in response to warm temperatures via expression of a thermosensitive cation channel, transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 (TRPV1) \[[24](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref024)\]. This channel transduces information about heat in the peripheral nervous system ([Box 1](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.box001)). TRPV1 channels are also activated by capsaicin, the chemical compound produced by hot peppers that causes the perception of heat \[[24](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref024)\]. However, the hypothesis that TRPV1 can depolarize POMC neurons is controversial. Previous studies showed little to no expression of TRPV1 receptors in the arcuate nucleus \[[25](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref025),[26](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref026)\]. Additionally, TRPV1 receptors have been shown to transduce temperatures at and above 42°C \[[24](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref024)\], much too high a body temperature for mammals, for which 37°C is normal and 38–39°C is elevated.

Finally, Jeong and colleagues asked whether POMC-mediated suppression of feeding occurred during physical exercise, a normal physiological state that raises both core body and brain temperature \[[34](http://journals.plos.org/plosbiology/article?id=10.1371/journal.pbio.2006188#pbio.2006188.ref034)\]. Mice were placed on a treadmill for 40 min to mimic a physiological state similar to cardiovascular exercise in humans. Indeed, this exercise raised temperature in the arcuate nucleus to 38–39°C and reduced subsequent feeding for the next hour compared with nonexercised mice. Importantly, the effects of exercise on appetite suppression were attenuated when the _Trpv1_ gene was deleted in POMC neurons or when αMSH receptors were blocked. Therefore, functional TRPV1 channels and POMC neuron signaling is required for exercise-induced suppression of food intake.

Taken together, the results of Jeong and colleagues demonstrate **that POMC neurons express functional TRPV1-like receptors, that these receptors increase POMC neuronal activity when temperature increases, and that functional activation of POMC neurons is necessary for appetite suppression in response to exercise-induced elevation of core body temperature.**

Additionally, core body temperature fluctuates according to an animal’s circadian rhythm, and it would be interesting to know if POMC-mediated suppression of appetite is influenced by core body temperature at various points during an animal’s active/inactive period. Consistently, _Pomc−/−_ mice show greatly reduced locomotor activity, an activity essential for foraging, during the active period"

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
**
So since this Reset, i've got POMC working in my hypothalamus again.

So now my T3 is working my core body temperature is actually hot or correct. It is highest from afternoon (peaks) until night.This could be upregulating the POMC via Transient receptor potential vanilloid 1 (TRPs) in my neurons.

Moreso than the above, the UV light hitting my eye is also creating POMC and then making B-endorphin.

The upregulation of POMC + Sunlight = appetite supression (via a-MSH upregulation) (tick) and opiate B-endorphins (tick)

How it is giving me dry mouth and a stoned feeling is to do with the Cannabinoid receptors that POMC has a lot of dealing with. I'm not sure the mechanism yet, but i'm sure it will come to light soon.**

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-246896>

Yes I do.

I spent January in India, March and April in Mexico, and had 2 months in this British heatwave may and June while unemployed.

Then July I’ve been at work but had my regular exposure. If you look back at the picture on the last page, I’m still very dark.

This whole journal has shown how just plain old sunbathing or Solar exposure hasn’t solved my problems. I think there are others on the forum who resonate with this too.

We all know it’s importance, it’s obviously catalysed my redox potential improvement and I have come on leaps and bounds, I feel the best in a while.

This skin rash. It’s something else. I think, and I mean I think, it’s Cortisol... and that it’s my Circadian Rhythm. Whenever I get to sleep before 9, my rash is like magically better.

My childhood = Blue light toxic @ night. I used to sleep with an LED on as i was scared of dark.

Age 7 I had a TV in my room and a PlayStation.

So from 7 to 21 I stayed up staring at a screen every night til past midnight.

Can you imagine how bad my eczema was as a kid?

I think I’ve found the deep cause of my issues. Circadian mismatch by blue light toxicity during childhood development.

John Ott’s Book health and light showed what happens to kids with blue light. Matthew Walkers book Why We Sleep also has a whole chapter on Sleep/REM and Autism/ADHD. I was diagnosed hyperactive borderline Autistic as a kid by 1 doc, another said I wasn’t... so was I?? I don’t know.

But it all starts to make sense.

I just gotta test the hypothesis now and be asleep before 9 every night, which is actually one of the hardest hacks I’ve tried. Modern house sharing life really gets in the way here.

I’m moving out in a month, own place and own bathroom. It will be a circadian fortress!

Exactly my point..... it brings up a lot of old frustration, which explains the rant! And it also symbolises my friends and family who have issues and are reluctant to listen to anything this site could offer them - but they blame it all on "stress", without even knowing what that is or why it happens!

Ok, so it's stress - have it your way. But what do you know of it? what are you going to do about it? The usual response is "pffft i don't know".

the only thing that stops me from preaching to these people is how i think they are on their own journey anyway - and they need to discover things for themselves, bit by bit... just like i've done. So I back away and let them continue on their journey, with respect.

It is hard though when one of your best friends has lost a father, a brother and a best friend to suicide for depression - and she's been on meds since 8 yrs old for the same thing - and she's depressed and another of her best friends is suicidal. Once a week i come home to her crying. It scares me! Her best friend has been trying to discover what the depression "is", the source. So she went to Peru on an Ayahuasca trip, Shaman and the pipes in a cave, and she's not got the answer she's looking for. She's home, and is "out of ideas" with how to tackle her depression, and "wants to die".

To here this, knowing what I know, is gut wrenchingly difficult.

I showed my close friend a documentary called "magic pill" about the ketogenic diet - i know we are ahead of that on this site, but i thought i'd get her ROS down and increase Electron flow down her membranes, lol. She bought it, and she's gone keto, and had some of the best weeks of her life - however, she doesn't journal, so when she crashes off a sugar binge or alcohol binge, she falls into a pit of hopelessness, as she doesn't understand - and believes keto to be failing her, when really she's failing keto!!

The sugar cravings and alcohol and substance abuse that she partakes in are all due to Leptin resistance and therefore circadian mismatch. She sits on a Macbook til late at night. She also says "i sleep for hours, but still feel tired, i'm always so so tired!!".

So, i though, i will show her the leptin reset. she eats a BAB, and feels better - no cravings.

But again, she fails after a week and collapses again.

then, i thought i would try to convince her of blue light and sleep..... i give her the Why We Sleep book, she reads 1 chapter, and fails to finish.

I keep trying all the time, but the fact is - she is so low dopamine that nothing will stick, she can't keep a routine for more than a week. It's reciprocal behavior.

Now she's talking of going to the doctor, for the same meds that have never helped her..... It's so hard sometimes!!!

Lab results back........ and my predictions of a "low T" are confirmed:

D.H.E.A. Sulphate 6.6 umol/L (0.44-13.4)

FOLLICLE STIM. HORMONE 4.4 IU/L (1.5-12.4)

LUTEINISING HORMONE 6.8 IU/L (1.7-8.6)

TESTOSTERONE 23.3 nmol/L (7.6-31.4)

**Free-Testosterone(Calculated) \*0.23nmol/L (0.30–1.00)

SEX HORMONE BINDING GLOB\*94nmol/L (16-55)**

17-Beta OESTRADIOL 76 pmol/L < 192

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* Previous labs for reference:

**HS CRP - 5.5 (0 - 5)
Progesterone - Less than 0.2 (0.7 - 4.7)**
Ferritin - 128 (30 - 400)

Reverse T3 - 17 (10 - 24)
TSH - 3.9 (0.27 - 4.2)
Free Thyroxine - 18 (12 - 22)
Free T3 - 3.1 (3.1 - 6.8)
Thyroglobulin antibodies - less than 10 (0 - 115)
Thyroid peroxidase antibodies - 20.4 (0 - 34)

B12 - 112 (25 - 165)
Folate - 9 (greater than 2)

I'm going to quote [@kris90](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19157/), as i know he has a lot to say about Free T and SHBG..... His views give me hope that i'm actually in a good state. I stopped CT'ing because I wanted to have minimal variables on this rash recovery, CT heals but the cause isn't a "lack of CT". But I plan to get back to it in September, when the temperatures here drop and i will be falling into a seasonal groove of cooler temps @ my surfaces with a lower amount of UV light.

These early bedtimes = morning wood @ 4am when i wake up, but if i don't do that i don't get it. Another reason to get my ass to bed @ 8pm - my reproductive tissues respond immediately.

I'm clearly producing Testosterone, quite a lot of it too, but SHBG is capturing all of it. Why?......

Here is Kris90 view:
**"Are you fasting, ketotic, doing CT and getting sun? If so, all of these things lower the release of sex hormones because they increase receptor binding affinity. That means less active (free) hormone is required to activate the androgen receptors.

Having high SHBG in this state is actually a good thing, because SHBG protects the androgen receptors from getting too much deuterium so they function better. It is a sign of superior metabolic flexibility and energy efficiency. That is why I have stopped chasing high T levels, because my lifestyle changes the biochemistry in my body to allow optimal receptor activity without the need of my testes producing massive amounts of hormone."**

Extract from Quantum Biology 2: Quantum PCOS

Here’s the cascade if everything is working properly:

-   Pregnenolone > Progesterone > remaining steroid (sex) hormones and Vitamin D.

Here’s the cascade if you’re stressed:

-   Pregnenolone > Cortisol > Liver, which makes more LDL because you’re not making steroid hormones (wash, rinse, repeat).

The Hypothalamus-Pituitary Axis (HPA) controls the entire hormone cascade. Why is this important? If you’re in the stress pathway – you can’t make Progesterone. If you’re not making Progesterone, you can’t enter the PPP, regardless of diet or cold!

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**So i guess i need to wait for my Cortisol to return, if it's messed up then it confirms the above.

But that is positive news... as it means i need to just control Cortisol via SLEEP. Which is exactly what is working for me.
**

### kris90 — 2018-08-01T14:21:56+0300

Cold showers definitely count. I take 2 a day year round, but I do tubs and snow bathing in winter, as well as spend a lot of time outdoors in -30 C in our Canadian winters.

Here's a couple things to note:
\-Cooling skin surface = increase electron density = have more magnetic "pull" for sunlight on your skin (DHA helps with this too because of its electron density)
\-Cooling skin surface not only increases our "pull" for sunlight, but it also increases heat flow from deep in our tissues (mitochondrial uncoupling). So you have heat flowing upward from tissues into blood, and heat coming down (from the sun - i.e. UVA light) and that puts the pinch on deuterium

^ That is how it works for a loosely coupled haplotype. There's more magnetism involved, because the electric fields from the sun are weaker at higher latitudes. For a tightly coupled haplotype, they have strong UV/IR light year round, so again, the UV light presses down on skin, the IR light gets absorbed in water of tissues, and the heat rises up. Water is basically a mirror for IR light, so that's how the Africans get their pinch from UV/IR and they don't need to release heat from mitochondria.

So we need to use cold to increase our magnetic field to get more driving force from the sun on our surface, and release more heat from our tissues to make the pinch.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-09T10:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-247990>

Update -

So I've had about 7 out of the last 8 days with sleep before 9pm - and all those days I've not scratched; the day I got to sleep @ 9.30pm I had a midnight flare up.

Everyday my eczema clears just a little bit more. My hands feels smooth now, but they still look a bit dry and red. Not quite there yet.

I've had it since a baby, but the hand rash has been here about 18 months. So i've got a lot of tissue damage to recover. I'm hoping in the next month or two that i can fully heal the tissue by regeneration in sleep.

One thing I've noticed, I can't sleep @ night before 9pm if i've had any carbs or fruits or any kind of "irritant" @ lunch or dinner. I can have them for breakfast, but no other time. They seem to cause a bit of inflammation in my gut, which raises Cortisol, and that spike keeps it trending higher at a later time, when it should be very low. So i'm very "wakeful" and "alert" @ 8pm. Without them (the last 5 days), I've found i have no issues dropping off @ 8pm or 8.30pm. I have however, been waking @ 1am and 3am, I'm still trying to hack that, but at least my skin is healing!

I've also cut the carbs and fruit in the morning (sorry [@drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) ) , and i've started eating 5 Oysters per day (smaller ones than the picture earlier in the journal!).

The impact this (cutting fruits altogether) has had on my libido has been immense. I think any fruits or irritants = inflammation (for me) in my gut, due to my history of IBD/Crohn's this would make sense. So the Pregnenolone is shunted to Cortisol for the anti-inflammatory affect on the gut, which leaves my Sex Steroids and Progesterone in a dump.

This all makes sense, if you see my recent lab tests = low free T and very low Progesterone (no pregnenolone to make them, shunted to Cortisol) and high HS-CRP (Inflammation raising the Cortisol).

So my Libido is literally firing up - I'm waking up horny and going to bed horny. This is something I never paid attention to in my wellness, i thought I was fine, but now I realise I wasn't at all!

One thing I find, is that without Carbs in the AM, just eggs + seaweed + onion + oysters and coconut oil, that I can get dehydrated and feel a bit of a lag by the Strong AM Sun we have here at the moment and the heat (and of course the EMF). This used to be my BIGGEST problem. I went to Bali and tried to do Keto in 35'C heat and just got so fatigued it ruined the trip, this was the story in Mexico too - which is why i started using the Salt hack. **The Sun deactivates Adrenaline, so if you are a shit Cortisol receptor or Low Cortisol then you will feel tired. This is the story of Mexico for me: I had terrible Cortisol + so I felt worse and more fatigued in the Sun. And Why i feel better in the UK when i got home and did the Leptin Rx, because it's focus is reducing Cortisol to reset communication with Hypothalamus and peripheral energy status.**

This lag it's only slight, barely noticeable, which shows how much my energy and metabolism has improved since Mexico and Bali. Still, I'm striving for more and I sense a little bit of Adrenal Fatigue just lingering... So i'm hoping, in a month, once the inflammation is right down and my sleep is perfect - that i won't get this "lag" at all. Alternatively, I will go BACK on the Leptin Reset to finish the job for another month or two. No problems there.

Today, it has rained in the morning in London for first time in about 3 months, it is a lot cooler, and I feel VERY energetic after my zero carb breakfast and no "lag" whatsoever. This gives me a lot of excitement for Winter - because if I can't win with the Sleep Hack this summer - then I know that CT this winter will absolutely sky rocket my wellness anyway.

Low testosterone and high estrogens promote arterial plaque buildup.
progesterone have some 5aR inhibitory ability.
You do not want more than (top laboratory level progesterone)(1.4ng/mL)
But less than that is not better. (P/E ratio)

Testosterone is converted to 5a-DHT with assistance of 5aR enzyme.
Good levels of 5a-DHT promote erections, libido.

Many foods that we eat are 5aR inhibitors (bad for sex, sometimes good for other health benefits).

Possibly the foods that you dropped were in category of 5aR inhibitors.

**List of 5α-reductase inhibitors**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List\_of\_5α-reductase\_inhibitors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_5%CE%B1-reductase_inhibitors)

**Herbs and other inhibitors**

..............

Interesting short list (fatty acids):
The relative inhibitory potencies of unsaturated fatty acids are, in decreasing order:
Gamma-Linolenic acid,
alpha-linolenic acid,
linoleic acid,
palmitoleic acid,
oleic acid, and
myristoleic acid.\[43\]

I've connected it to DHA intake but also to circadian peaks for endorphins, which are opiates, which occurs @ 2-4pm.

I have made it Blog number **#134 EMF 2: Einstein Meet Leptin.**

And I've found this extract about memory which I really like:

**How might memory really work?**
Science does not know how memory works. I think I do. Here is why: the human brain can also slow the
charge, current, or electron flow, using alterations in cerebral auto-regulation to different areas of the brain.
It can also decrease conductivity by changing the amount of CSF in the head based upon circadian cycles.
This is often why pseudo tumor cerebri is found in over weight females. This is due to electron loss and and
increase of CSF production by an altered clock. We can see radical improvements in their headache, if we
alter their chronobiology first. No one does, but I did, and it and it works. By decreasing blood flow, using
higher levels of CO2 by hyper-ventiltion, and increases in eNOS in the blood, the brain dramatically
decrease cerebral blood flow in parts of the Circle of Willis and the circulatory system around our
hippocampus to allow us to access memories of yester-years in a previous time. **By modulating the
electron flow in the mitochondria of neurons, we can go back in time, to an older reality.** This, I believe, is
the basis for human memory and our unique ability to plan for change to change our own reality, in my
opinion.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**Is my increase in Electrons with my 1:1 06/03 ratio and high DHA intake over the last few months starting to increase Electron Flow in my neurons and run electrons down synapses that I have forgotten - to take me back to smells and experiences as a 6 year old?**

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-13T11:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248343>

Went home for the weekend, Friday and Saturday night, back at my Moms house in the countryside.

Still got all the wifi and alexa and bad lights etc. Haven't fully convinced her about Blue light + EMF yet!

But I noted the Best Sleep i've had in months..... I slept from 8.30pm until 4.30am straight through, 8 hours solid hours. Again, no scratching. The Saturday night i did similar but woke a little earlier and snoozed in a little later. Again, no scratching.

But what was different was that as well as the Absence of a "flare up" or scratching of the skin, I actually HEALED the skin even more than I do in London when I sleep before 9. It wasn't Dry when i woke up, it looked completely normal, even the redness wasn't there. My Mom was absolutely astonished. That much so, that she has decided to give the "Why we sleep?" book a read and wants to re-arrange her schedule to sleep before 9.

Another thing was my libido and morning glory as we call it in the UK (is that a term in the US?) - well anyway, the libido was the best it's been so far. I also relaxed a huge amount, when i arrived friday night to when i left sunday afternoon I had completely relaxed and I felt really really good, despite it raining all weekend!

I get back to my mouldy london house and sleep from 8.30 til 2.30am wake up, then snooze til 5am. Why can't I sleep straight through here? The likely culprit is the nnEMF. But I also think it is the Mould (which proliferates uncontrollably under high nnEMF).

I had no scratching, but there it was again this morning - the dryness on my hands. So my hands are now more dehydrated from a night sleep in this house, than just the day before in my Mom's house.

Can't wait to move out - 6 weeks!!

The final thing i've found, since this Reset back in May, i've LOST SO MUCH WEIGHT. And since i've got the sleep down before 9, and my hunger is controlled, i'm literally zero % body fat. I look Lean as fuck, but a little too skinny for my liking. I've really tried to battle with the illusion of Mens Magazines/ Bodybuilding websites that i need to look like Arnie. But I'm also aware that i can just be "too thin", and that it might indicate an issue in the body. "Skinny LR folks" Jack calls them in his early blogs.

He says, "eat more raw cream and berries, cover your hash browns in butter" for the skinny LR guys. Well, I shouldn't be LR anymore, my labs all indicate a perfect thyroid and my appetite has gone and i feel energy, no adrenal fatigue, and i don't get crashes after food (IR). But I haven't added that muscle mass yet.

I hate talking about carbs and fats and calories. With all we know in this forum, i try to think of things more simply - in terms of the physics or thermodynamics. In one sense, I have condensed my matter and more Light = less Mass on the E=MC2 reversed equation. So being this skinny/ lean hunter gatherer type body is a good sign? But can't skinny bodies also be reflective of not enough light too? Like Anorexia, is surely lack of light, it's the oppposite form to obesity.

My non-kruse friends and family just keep saying "Exercise" and "weight lifting" - but i know that's not the answer. It always has to be SIMPLE.

Part of me believes it is my sleep - as Growth Hormone at 3-5am is where we grow and build muscle whilst burning fat. In London, as i said above, i usually wake at 2 or 3am and light sleep until 4 or 5am. Maybe this part of my sleep, which was so efficient at my Moms house where i slept through, is where i'm losing the ability to Grow and build muscle?

Maybe the eczema is controlled by the earlier phases of sleep, 8.30pm til 1/2am, where im out cold and deep in sleep - hence the eczema recovery. And the muscle building/growth hormone stage is at 3am til 5am, where im effectively half awake and snoozing.

In the meantime, I've started eating Raw Organic Chocolate Butter + Raw Organic Cacao Paste + Raw Organic Cacao Nibs + 60g Coconut Oil + Cinnamon + Sea Salt + Nutmeg. I just melt it all in a pan and then add the Nibs and eat/drink with a spoon while still warm. It's so amazing, and it doesn't give my Gut any Stress - i show no physical signs of inflammation or irritation. So it is a perfect "Weight gainer" for a leaky gut skinny guy.

30g ROC Butter (270 kcal) + 30g ROC Paste (240 kcal) + 60g CO (540 kcal) = 1050 calories. That should be putting "weight" on to me, but if anything i've gotten THINNER since starting it! All those Electrons in Raw 100% Chocolate making my mass decrease even more?? C2 increases, M decreases and E remains contant, right?

and I've had 5 oysters for breakfast - and nope, still feel like shit.

I'm considering arranging to move out my house this month, instead of next month. The reason I want to stay is because i've been able to sleep before 9pm and even though i have wake ups through the night in this house, the early bedtime is almost "protective" of those disruptions, and i wake up feeling fine. However, if i miss that 9pm window, then i'm subject to a night of scratching and a terrible night's sleep.

At my moms house at the weekend, i slept straight through from 8.30 til 4.30 and had the best 2 days of Libido/ cognitive function/ eczema in years...

I think the Mold/EMF in this house is clearly a big obstacle for me.

### Jenelle — 2018-08-16T12:15:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248587>

[@Sean Waters](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19464/) I'm sorry to hear you are having a bad day. I have had days where I just had to push through, SO looking forward to bedtime... and knowing that after a good night of sleep, everything will be better. It sucks, but at least it gives me something to look forward to, on a day like that.

And something I've thought about many times lately ~ and I know you'll be able to relate to: I've realized that \*good days\* are actually MY NORM now, and the crappy ones are rare, which makes them that much more noticeable.

This is in contrast to living in a crappy overly-emotional keyed-up state all the time ... and having the occasional really awesome day here and there, where I thought, "man, if I could just feel this way all the time!" or "what is different about today, so I can replicate it?!"

It's flip-flopped. And THAT is HUGE.

I also do find that I can literally plug my body into the earth, barefoot in the sun, and I picture it like a phone going on its charger ~ and I've described it that way to my kids. I hope you are able to do that today, even if it's just for a few minutes at a time.

### Sean Waters — 2018-08-17T10:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-optimal-journal.20757/post-248669>

Got to sleep just before 9pm - Woke @ 3am! But felt REFRESHED. Then I gave myself an extra 90 min REM cycle and got up @ 4.45am... Feel WAY better today.

No eczema, no cognition dysfunction, I happy switched on and ready to go with work today. Yesterday just seems like a bad dream...

THE POWER OF SLEEP!!!

What is significant is that I only slept 6-7 hrs total. Which is meant to be "not enough" according to conventional wisdom. But I keep reading, without any citations, "you can get all your NREM and REM sleep needs in 4 hour window between 8pm to Midnight".

Is that why I'm "protected" by disruptions? Is that why i'm able to hijack my immunity and stop my skin from flaring? Is that why 6 hours of sleep from before 9pm = complete rejuvenation?

Something else I have noticed too:

When I wake up @ 3am, if I start thinking too much and getting even the tiniest bit stressed - I start to feel an itch on my skin. Today I simply just deep breath in and out and relaxed my whole body and the itch completely disappeared.

Could this be, Cortisol raised at the WRONG Circadian time = Auto-immunity/ Eczema at that time?

Could Cortisol being raised, way before it's rhythmic time @ around sunset, cause immune cells to become over-active, or dysfunctional?
