Family and friends and hospice could only do so much for her or me. What they could do was greatly appreciated though. Perhaps it was a good for everyone that her dying process did not go on longer. I was exhausted at the end. I've never been so tapped.
>
> The reason that I am writing this is that I know other people in our community have experienced the death of a spouse (or child) but probably most have not. It seems like this is one of the most traumatic events you can go through. Stress can come from many sources but for most of us being close to intimate death near us is probably the top of the list. How one deals with it literally shapes the remaining life you have. I am in my 60's so I have to see this as an opportunity to recover and rebuild my life but only with concerted effort based on some sort of personal plan or strategy.
>
> I used every oppurtunity I could think of to be in nature a la Jack Kruse especially during the roughest time. Every morning I did grounding, watched the sun rise and followed circadian cycle as much as possible. But of course my emotional and mental energy was being dissipated quickly at the same time. The flower pot had many holes in it. My dopamine social network became extremely important to me. How I related to those important people in my life became more important to me than ever. I am not a highly social person by nature but I did not hold back. Some people I had known but did not considered part of my list, quite unexpectedly rose in rank. Our families do not live nearby but they all came once and sometime twice in those few weeks. Some local friends became super friends. My gratitude is great as is my strenghtened connection to them.
>

I did not see this coming. Perhaps she sensed it a number of months before. On the other hand, poor choices will often make for bad outcomes. Being educated, thoughtful and making better choices might help us avoid these extremes of suffering. I see that as the main purpose of the Jack Kruse community. Reinventing your body and your life has endless possibilities if you remain focused. What we want and how far we are willing to go in our life is an open book.

Thanks for sharing this.

### Jonathin — 2018-09-03T17:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jons-journal.20763/post-250048>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=250039)
>
> I wonder if she did not feel anything before the diagnosis? Like was she tired often, could she sleep, etc.?
>
> Are there no warning signs, before one get cancer?
> Or are we just so good in ignoring them?

I think the later. Denial can be subtle enough to largely be ignored by the conscious mind. Looking back I think she knew on some level that she was declining. The scary thing is that I see some similar signs in other people of my age group that I am close to and I am uncomfortable about their resignation. I am inclined to resist my aging process as much as possible.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233507>

As of 2015, Boros was investigating the possibility that Deuterium transfer to fat may be the best marker of mitochondrial failure -- is this currently accepted in Epi-Paleo land as the best proy for heteroplasmy?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-26T14:32:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233533>

As time elapses from birth one thing is clear.....mitochondrial likes DDW more. The higher the metabolic rate of the tissue the more DDW that tissue requires. I don't think the fractionation testing as it stands is a good measure. I have another test in mind as a proxy

### JanSz — 2018-01-26T16:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-deuterium-levels-best-proxy-for-heteroplasmy.20764/post-233548>

\=====================

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=233533)
>
> As time elapses from birth one thing is clear.....mitochondrial likes DDW more. The higher the metabolic rate of the tissue the more DDW that tissue requires.

Does that mean that content of D in matrix water varies?
How much?
\------------
Metabolic water∼115 ppm (D/H)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/deuterium-in-foods.20423/page-7#post-229480)
[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19\_Supplement/1426](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/74/19_Supplement/1426)
names there László G. Boros, Gábor Somlyai
Metabolic water by complete fat oxidation, coupled with cytochrome-c, contains low average ∼115 ppm (parts per million) deuterium due to deuterium discrimination by plant lipogenic enzymes during photosynthesis.

### Melisa — 2018-01-27T02:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fighting-cancer-101.20767/post-233592>

Hello I'm 44 year old female diagnosed with endometrial cancer. A week ago I had a complete hysterectomy. The DR believes it was all contained in the uterus with no spread to the lymph nodes. I've been practicing IF and Keto for over a year, work out 4 times a week and take supplements ( D3, Mag, Adrenal support, biotin, K3). I'm introducing Curcumin Supreme , B Supreme with probiotics to combat the current pain meds. I ordered Blublox glasses to help with my mitochondria going forward. Since my surgery sleep is a major issue.

Can anyone give me other advice or directions I can take to further heal and combat this cancer?

Time to biohack my body and push it.

Much appreciated.

# Thread: My Optimal Journal 2018
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 85
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alison N — 2018-01-27T05:54:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-233602>

Update to my original post:

\------------------------------
Maternal Haplotype: H1M

Paternal Haplotype: R-L51

**Family History:**

**Father:** I do not know my father’s side of anything except that he had issues with eczema.

**Mother:** Osteopenia (has had several bad car and accidents). Arthritis from nerve compression.

**Maternal Grandmother:** Rheumatoid Arthritis. She cannot tolerate any medications, painkillers, etc. It makes her very nausea. Breast Cancer – “spontaneous remission” after a year. AMD + Cataracts.

No other known diseases.

**Genetic Carrier:**

Hereditary Hematomachrosis (1 allele)

Age Related Macular Degeneration (2 alleles)

**Genetic SNPS:**

SH2B3 +/+

COMT rs4633 +/+

COMT rs4680 +/+

MAO-A +/+

CBS +/+

MTRR rs1801394 +/+

_DAO ALL +/+_

RAG1 +/+

SOCS1 +/+

_GAD ALL +/+_

PEMT +/+

TCN1 +/+

SULTA2 +/+

GSTP1 rs1695 +/-

FTO rs17817449 +/-

FTO rs9939609 +/-

FTO rs1121980 +/-

FTO rs142085 +/-

CYP2R1 +/-

CYP2R1 rs12794714 +/-

BDNF +/-

Vitamin D Binding Protein rs2282679 +/-

Vitamin D Binding Protein rs7041 +/-

FAD51 rs174548 +/-

FAD51 rs1535 +/-

PEMT +/-

CYPA12 +/-

NAT 2 +/-

BHMT +/-

MTRR rs1802509 +/-

SOD2 +/-

FOLR1 +/-

NOS1 +/-

FOXE1 +/-

Mitochondrial function:

SLC19A1 +/-

NDUFS3 +/-

COX5A +/-

COX6C +/-

SULTA1 +/-

**Bio:**

31 years old. I have lived in Portland, Oregon for the past 5 years. My husband and I moved from the MD & DE area near Ocean City, MD where we lived for 3-4 years prior. Prior to that, I grew up in MI since my early teenage years. My childhood was mostly in MD, PA & DE. I grew up taking many antibiotics (my mom is a nurse). I had two bouts of mononucleosis very young and Herpes Simplex eruptions from stress and ate the wrong foods throughout my youth. I was also a C-section delivery.

I was chubby child then lost weight by working myself out to death and trying veganism. I had a lot of energy prior to that last year and was always on the beach, swimming and out in the sun, exercising. I did eat A LOT of large fish like Albacore Tuna. Eventually, I experimented with Veganism for a year and around this time I got sick (was also working three jobs) The last 6-months of living on the East Coast we lived in a musty house near the water that had visible mold and I experienced a lot of emotional stress. From this point into the first year of living in Portland, I felt the worst.

I have had a low thyroid since 2003-2004 but never took medication for it. Hashimoto’s with antibodies around 33 (resolved autoimmunity with diet)

I also had an ATV accident in 2005ish and had a concussion. A few years later, it revealed mild to moderate degenerative disc disease and a small syrinx that is stable in my thoracic spine. I experience a lot of pain from this. I also had two other car accidents that caused cervical stiffening and shoulder pain. A brain MRI in 2009 showed a 0.3cm “white matter lesion.” Wanting a functional MRI follow-up.

Originally **DX’d with Mercury Poisoning** (Chris Shade’s lab had to extend their own graph because limits were over them). Removed three mercury amalgam fillings with a biological dentist. During this time, I had neuropathy and my entire right leg would go numb walking down the street. I successfully chelated to very low levels over 1-2 years. Then chronic fatigue set in, had all the **positive mold labs** including the HLA-mold-susceptible genotype. **Also positive for Lyme (2015)**. I moved in 2016 from one very bad apartment (within 15 minutes I would be completely stupefied in that environment) to a new one.

_Over 2014-2016 I’ve tried various treatments but nothing worked except ozone (rectal) therapy – which turned the lights back on in a big way, but after a few weeks, the effects fizzled out (environment sucked). It also became incredibly expensive. I have stopped taking any supplements at this point because I am focusing on the environment and Jacks’ recommendations. Vitamin D level has been between 33-40 consistently. I felt the best over the summer in 2017 as I began spend WAY more time in the sun._

**Current Symptoms:**

Numbness and tingling in hands and feet. I went to the ER in November 2017 for an episode of total left hand to elbow numbness but the EKG was normal (did not run labs) and the doc mentioned it sounded like MS to him. Ironically, I was standing at my un-grounded and unprotected laptop at the time.

· Brain fog, fatigue, anxiety.
· On-going and intermittent periods of apathy and lethargy, intermixed with days of great enthusiasm and energy.
· IBS – had constipation forever, which after introducing Iodine caused tremendous loose stools, which has since stabilized between the two.
· Eczema on my right hand only (gets better with Megaspore probiotics) – recently just resolved a bit after eating a lot of nuts and peanut butter (which I NEVER usually eat), but the cravings were intense. It still primarily itches at nighttime.
· Cold hands and feet.
· I occasionally get “lightning strike” like whips of pain across my torso – completely random.
· Dry and itchy eyes at night – sometimes tearing.

**Home + Work Situation:**

Third floor apartment. Feeling better after moving out but still significant issues. Live with spouse who snores, loudly, all the time. I have chronically disturbed sleep and tend to awaken 12:00 am – 2:00 am nightly to switch rooms. This is not because I have trouble staying asleep but the noise wakes me up. Interestingly, on vacation, he sometimes will not snore at all.

I’ve turned off the WIFI on my laptop and the WIFI on our Frontier router isn’t emitting a signal whenever I’m home; however, my husband turns it on because he’s addicted to his cellphone and manages lots of people remotely.. (Despite me trying to educate him). When it is on, I am on the other side of the room. It is in the range of 0.30-0.1. In the bedroom, it is 0.1-0.05. My phone is on airplane mode 90% of the time.

Body voltage measurements in the bedroom is .887 – 1.33 and floats around 1.02 with the bed pulled from the wall. We have never had any lamps plugged in nor any electronics while we sleep. We have the breaker panel in the bedroom but after turning them all off and back on, the electric readings jump from 3.445 so they stay on. I primarily reside in my apartment where the electric levels are lowest at 0.445 – 0.673 and the RF I cannot get below 0.10. It is USUALLY at .30 most of the time.

I have not yet measured any magnetic frequencies.

I have a BioMat but body voltage measures over 3.025 so I don’t use it anymore – and it makes me very tired after (redox is shot).

I work at a Fertility Clinic and I work on a computer for 10 hours – three days a week and half a day on Saturdays. The last time I measured, it was 0.30 and now it is through the roof 3 - 6.00 – the levels change according to how my co-workers use their cell phones… so my job is killing me…. WOW.. I will be trying to my hardest to figure out a new income stream so I can quit.

I do use Indoor Bluetech glasses the entire day I work, when I’m lucky to work on the third floor we have a huge glass window that gives natural light (though it is UV tinted) the EMF’s aren’t quite as bad. I use the orange Uvex and a special made BPI 550 tinted lense together as soon as the sun goes down.

**Diet:**

I am now recently following the Leptin RX more strictly. No grains, beans, processed sugars or heavy carbohydrates. I am always craving high fat foods, love bacon, pork, beef (grassfed). I love seafood and get wild and fresh when I can but it’s mostly canned Wild Salmon, smoked oysters (and raw), canned sardines, clams and frozen wild scallops and octopus occasionally. I have pea protein powder and exogenous ketone powder on hand – mostly because I can’t always afford the best food.

I also eat a ton of coconut products but have tried to hold off for summer months. I buy many green leafy vegetables from the Asian markets, but I am guessing this is a circadian mismatch because gai-lin & bokchoy definitely do not grow indigenously in Oregon…. **Would these be okay in the summer, despite the fact they do not grow where I am?** _Jack mentioned he eats coconut in the summer months, but there not native to New Orleans either…._

I just started add duck fat to my coffee and it is surprisingly good.

I eat many alternative yogurts like coconut, almond and cashew as I cannot do dairy but I love yogurt and it seems to be a good source of cultured food. I also use a lactic acid ferment to make my own kraut.

I drink a lot of green tea of various types – I’ve been boiling each batch for 90 seconds as Jack recommends but I drink about 40 oz. of tea a day.. I just crave the tea.. I drink coffee occasionally.

I have a counter-top RO water machine (AquaTru) and I add minerals to it.

Constantly craving sea salt.

Constantly craving nuts and nut butters.

Always thirsty despite drinking 65-80 oz. of filtered water a day.

**Allergies:**

Gold

Shrimp (acute allergy)

Chicken eggs (duck eggs are good)

Wheat

Dairy – would like to experiment with the cultured ghee but afraid it might cause autoimmune issues – molecular mimicry?

**Goals:**

Find a competent doctor to assist in progress towards optimal.

Fix REDOX.

Heal leaky gut and increase diversity of flora.

I weigh approximately 145-150 lbs. I would like to use 15-20 lbs.

Gain muscle in upper back.

**Protocols:**

Following the Leptin RX – doing CT about 3-4x a week. My bath tub in the winter is 52-57 degrees so I don’t add ice. I am working up from 10-15 minutes to longer. My energy sharply increases when I do it – sometimes I cannot sleep because my mind gets very racey. I always shiver - If I do it in the AM, I do actually get hungry. (Jack said it usually diminishes hunger).

Implementing the Mitochondrial RX and Osteoporosis RX soon.

My biggest hardship is financial and needing to wait to get labs drawn, etc – things move more slowly than I would like

Will be posting updated labs soon.

Last BUN/Creatinine Ratio: 14

TSH: 5.3

FT4: 1.0

FT3: 2.0

\--------------

**QUESTIONS
**
For the Leptin RX - Is it okay to eat within 30 minutes of rising if it is technically 5:30-6 am and it is still dark? Or is it better to wait until the sun rises?

>
> _I live on the third floor of an apartment building. I’ve turned off our WIFI router (occasionally its on because of husband) but when it is I’m the farthest away. The lowest I can get it to on the Accousticom meter is a fluctuation between 0.30-0.10. I’ll usually sit on the floor that measures the lowest part of the house. ELF electric frequencies measure .9-1.3 in the bedroom. I’ve pulled the bed as far away as the wall as I can and it’s still between these levels — it changes depending if neighbors are using their washing machines, etc. Husband snores… so I move to the living room floor which electrically, measures .5-.6 volts.. lower than actual bed area. Everything around me in unplugged. I find I can actually dream when I sleep on the floor in living area- however, I’m CHRONICALLY interrupted at 12-1:30 am to move from my bed to somewhere else.. then I have a routine of turning off WIFI,, silencing his cell phones, making the “bed” .. so on. Not optimal, but not sure what else to do? He has his own blue light toxicity issues, fatty liver and workaholism issues to work out - I’ve tired to spread the knowledge but it’s not appreciated .. yet. Like Jack says, fix yourself and then become a beacon of light for others._
>
> _I work 32 hours a week at a fertility clinic, 10 hour days Wed-Saturday. This is sedentary, computer work. I have indoor blue techs while I work and use blue blocking glasses as soon as the sun goes down - I’ve been doing this since August 2017 - EMFs where I sit at work measure 0.3 - 1. I have yet to measure electronic frequencies nor have I yet measured magnetic.. (to come)…_
>
> _I had mononucleosis twice in high school - I grew up eating SAD - fast food, allergenic foods - worked out like crazy in high school, lost fat, but then went vegan. Lost menstrual cycles.
>
> Fast forward, 20 - 25 years old I lived in Ocean City, MD - was able to work three jobs, slept probably 4 hours a night - had great energy, but I was always on the beach, in the sun, in the ocean, working out.. Never knew where I got the energy from and how I could be as active as I WAS … after 2 years of cycling through that.. we moved into apartment that had visible black mold and smelt “damp” and musty.. after a summer in that house, things got weird and stressful… so we moved to the west coast._
>
> _Apartment living - diagnosed with mold toxicity - very moldy apartment - I diagnosed myself with Lyme via urine PCR - quit all doctors because their advice wasn’t working. Then I stumbled upon Dr. Kruse’ work and I’ve been implementing it ever since._
>
> _As of 2017 - the #1 thing that helped me the most has been Iodine… I’m taking 87.5 mg right now. I eat seafood, crave fats, ketogenic diet & hardly any sugar. I follow a seasonal eating approach… the vitamin D / sunshine is an issue right now…. not sure whether to invest in the Fiji Sperti Lamp or temporarily tan.. or just wait it out until Summer…_
>
> _My next step is implementing the Mitochondrial RX but wondering if my nnEMF environment is too high for it to be useful or futile? Our lease ends in October 2018 but financially speaking, not sure what we’ll do._
>
> _In school to become a scientist, have always been interested in health, but money is a real issue. Things move VERY slowly health wise because of this._
>
>
> **_DIAGNOSES_**
>
> _DDD: Thoracic Spine. Cervical Spine: loss of lordosis and multiple whiplash car accidents._
>
> _Lyme Disease._
>
> _Hypothyroid: as of Jan 2018_

>

>
> _I also had a concussion back in 2005. I had an ATV accident - hit a tree at 30 mph and flipped off landing on my stomach - lost consciousness for about 15 seconds; never saw a doctor being a dumb teenager. Later I saw treatment and a thoracic MRI revealed DDD (see attached) and today I have a lot of pain. Brain MRI of 2008 showed small white matter lesion. Brain fog has increased exponentially since then. Numbness/tingling in hands/feet. Some say its neuropathy from disc compression and others say its a progression towards MS._
>
> _CT works to relieve back pain but I wan’t to reverse it. Is that possible? Working on building more muscle._
>
> _I just bought a bamboo privacy fence for my balcony on the third floor. I’m about to get the inflatable tub to CT on my deck to take the treatment even deeper._
>
> _The only supplement I’m on is Iodine with Selenium, Magnesium, C, Zinc, B - the co-factors, that’s about it._
>
>
> _**My maternal haplotype: H1M — Don’t know Paternal.**_
>
> _As of today, I seem to have issues with “good days” and “bad days” - One day I have great enthusiasm and energy the next day I’m fried and apathetic. I could easily sleep 12-14 hours on those days. Poor exercise endurance._
>
> _I have a lot of GI issues - I know there’s leaky gut and dysbiosis. Just submitted a Urine Organic Acid.. wanted to perform a Viome; anyone here do that yet? Will be working on repairing gut with specific pre and probiotics. Recently went to the Philippines and haven’t quite felt the same since… Most likely picked up a protozoa from freshwater. I had strange rashes after several swimming incidents._
>
> _Next steps are implanting the Mitochondrial RX - I’m still listening to 2012, 2013 webinars (I know, lots to catch up on)._
>
> _I’mw working on building up muscle to stabilize my back and reduce pain. I’m trying to best to mitigate the nnEMF situation. Ideally quit my job and move out of this apartment but in the interim, I’m either outside in the park reading or in the apartment in the lowest radiation area)._

_I'm 30 years old.. will be 31 tomorrow on the 27th._

I live in Portland, OR
maternal haplotype: H1M

Algeria, Morocco, Tunisia, Libya and Mauritania.
So your optimal latitude is in tropics.

\-----------------------------------------------------------
TSH 5.9 -
FT3 2.5

drink Gerolsteiner and Mountain Valley Water.
diagnosed with mold toxicity
diagnosed myself with Lyme via urine PCR
DDD Degenerative Disc Disease
Brain MRI of 2008 showed small white matter lesion. Brain fog has increased exponentially
others say its a progression towards MS.
Husband snores…
The only supplement I’m on is Iodine with Selenium, Magnesium, C, Zinc, B - the co-factors
I have a lot of GI issues - I know there’s leaky gut and dysbiosis.
\----------------------------------------------------------------
replace Gerolsteiner and Mountain Valley Water with half and half (Aquafina) and (Preventa-25)
Aquafina is sold everywhere, just google
Preventa-25 get from CignatureHealth.com
From CignatureHealth get tests Breathy and Urine test, do them before you start drinking Preventa
afterward do those tests every 1-2 months until your test results stabilize at somewhat below 130ppm
Consider drinking (Aquafina) and (Preventa-25) as 99% of your water intake for long time.

Eat lots of fat, pork and beef lard, butter, coconut oil they will lower your deuterium.
Egg yolks are low in deuterium
Do not drink coconut water it is high in deuterium.
Solids in the green salads you eat are low in deuterium, but juice made of it is high.

Aquafina made by Pepsi is the only known to me RO water that was tested and found low in deuterium (139.5ppp (D/H), that is much much lower than any known to me natural waters (that usually are very expensive).
I have send for testing RO water sold by Costco, will publish results when get them. That water is the cheapest that I know of.

Body makes matrix water out of the fat you eat. 1 volume of fat make 1.1 volume of water.
When you eat enough fat your need for drinking water should be less.
Think of 1.5 -2 liters/day

Eat Redmont salt, at least 2 teaspoons a day (but no more than 12 tablespoons (twelve Tbsp).
It eases kidneys energy use, and adjusts positively many micronutrients.
Body, when adjusting to low available salt recirculates existing salt, that makes kidneys work overtime.
Up to 60% energy used by kidneys may be spend on that work.

KIE of D to O, N, and C.
(hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, carbon)
(less common) isotopes are used by our bodies to adjust biological processes

ELF electric frequencies measure .9-1.3 in the bedroom. I’ve pulled the bed as far away as the wall as I can and it’s still between these levels — it changes depending if neighbors are using their washing machines, etc. Husband snores… so I move to the living room floor which electrically, measures .5-.6 volts.. lower than actual bed area. Everything around me in unplugged. I find I can actually dream when I sleep on the floor in living area- however, I’m CHRONICALLY interrupted at 12-1:30 am to move from my bed to somewhere else.. then I have a routine of turning off WIFI,, silencing his cell phones, making the “bed” .. so on. Like Jack says, fix yourself and then become a beacon of light for others._

I have indoor blue techs while I work and use blue blocking glasses as soon as the sun goes down - I’ve been doing this since August 2017 - EMFs where I sit at work measure 0.3 - 1. I have yet to measure electronic frequencies nor have I yet measured magnetic.. (to come)…_
>
> _I had mononucleosis twice in high school - I grew up eating SAD - fast food, allergenic foods - worked out like crazy in high school, lost fat, but then went vegan. Lost menstrual cycles.
>
> Fast forward, 20 - 25 years old I lived in Ocean City, MD - was able to work three jobs, slept probably 4 hours a night - had great energy, but I was always on the beach, in the sun, in the ocean, working out.. Never knew where I got the energy from and how I could be as active as I WAS … after 2 years of cycling through that.. we moved into apartment that had visible black mold and smelt “damp” and musty.. after a summer in that house, things got weird and stressful… so we moved to the west coast._
>

>

I had strange rashes after several swimming incidents._

### Alison N — 2018-02-02T00:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-233938>

**VDR Snps**

VDR Taq +/+
VDR Fok 1 +/+
VDR BSM -/-

**"2-fold increase in risk of developing discopathies (such as herniated disc) and/or osteochondrosis (derangement of bone growth), while the A allele was protective."**
**
This was really eye opening...
**
_VDR Taq I polymorphism may be associated with susceptibility to Oral Squamous Cell Cancer......so avoid booze and tobacco and you should be OK. This SNP links VDR to NK T cell activation. I would also say I would pushes curcumin and K2 with this SNP. It is also associated with periodontal disease......so it is linked to heart disease too......if their HCY is up make sure it is not above 12. If it is they need expensive methylation testing done. Why the oral cancer link? Viral and heavy metal toxicity because when VDR Taq 1 is +/+.....it changes how COMT enzyme behaves. COMT is all about dopamine clearance. These two genes work together so the status of one will affect the other. When you combine the SNP's you can actually act more like a COMT -/- (not good at all). This is why further testing is needed.

When COMT is double negative or there are sequential SNP making it behave like you are COMT -/- it implies that the enzyme is working very well and is rapidly breaking down dopamine. This means you are using up methyl groups (BH4) rapidly and there is less available for other physiologic needs. This drives your HCY higher and it can down regulate immunity, especially T cell function. You might have to add methyl donors in supplement form for optimal dopamine and serotonin balance and for optimal methyl balance in the body.

In addition, since phase two detox requires methyl groups for heavy metal clearance you are more susceptible to toxic heavy metals in the mouth or diet and your viral/bacterial immunity will be altered. It will take longer to excrete metals and clear infection and this chronic immunity down regulation also leads to cancer/infections.

VDR Fok ++ = VDR (Fok-I) polymorphism is associated with the risk of bladder cancer and SLE (lupus) for many of the same reasons above. VDR gene Fok I polymorphism has been associated with SLE in the Chinese population. The SLE patients carrying F allelle Â± are more likely to have serositis and produce anti-dsDNA antibody, anti-Sm antibody and anti-Histone antibody, presumably as a result of down-regulation of VDR mRNA. So here you would need to see if you are making AI antibodies which are also directly tied to T Cell function. Look for lowered CD4/8 ratio's and elevated HCY above 12 or below 7. This SNP seems to have very different expression in different populations. It is bad in Asians but not so bad in Indians and we do not know why as yet. That is all I know.........
_

### Sheddie — 2018-02-14T18:05:44+0200

> [Alison N said:](/goto/post?id=233602)
>

>

Hello Alison, You have a lot to contend with but I think your 'sleuthing' guided by the new paradigm is getting to the bottom of identifying such hefty environmental onslaughts... One by one corrections will yield encouraging results.

You get my applause on your observations while using the "Bio-Mat." If it's the same manufacturer I had one, over 5 years ago now, and the plug on the mat, the one right near the cranium, tested very badly on a TriField meter. I had a few months' fight with the company CEO from Korea about the issue, but I did finally get authorization to return it for refund. So, I would say this -- IF the manufacturer hasn't yet corrected the known harm/design flaw in their product they should be eviscerated in public warning channels!

I kept my correspondence so it would be evidence of my report on the issue with my single purchased mat. I didn't go very cyber-wide to publicize the issue but I did complain to Mark Sircus who was at the time strongly promoting and recommending these mats. I did not purchase my Bio-Mat from him. So, I wondered how long you had your mat before you noticed it wasn't as 'health-supporting' as it might have claimed? If it's newer than, say, 4 years or so I should think you could be able to return it...? PM me if I can help with that. (The specific item I had was a Richway far-infrared Amethyst Bio-mat.)

Best luck furthering the very good progress you're making in analyzing the problems that, at your age, are simply heart-breaking.

Hello Sheddie!

Thank you for your response and kind words! I am so sorry to hear about your experience with the Biomat. 5 years of use with bad ratings... that is very, very upsetting. How did it make you feel initially? Were all the levels bad or was it one field more so than another? The electric levels were above what I wanted. The RF levels were on the lower end but still showed a response; even with their built in EMF reduction. I don't yet have a magnetic meter, so I'm curios what levels you found there? It sounds like we have the same mat. I purchased it last year so perhaps they fixed this issue. Since I haven't measured the magnetic field I just haven't taken the chance and unless all of the fields show a reduction in a new environment, I'd be very hesitant to use it again. So it just sits until I figure out what to do with it..

I had used it off/on for a few months, a couple of times a week. I had read that feeling bad or excessively tired was a sign of poor REDOX but I wasn't sure what to make of it.. so I pushed through until I started measuring. Lesson learned for me. That's part of the lesson I'm continually learning the more I experiment, however, but it beats doing nothing - and certainly better than getting poor advice from those who push unsavory advice in the name of the profit.

Alison, I might have written a possible misunderstanding -- I have since checked and I bought it in 10/2009, queried about the EMF issue in early Spring , wrangled about an exchange, and finally allowed to return it for refund in 7/2010 -- so it was some 9 months of back & forth with the company.

each person

### Alison N — 2018-03-23T23:43:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-237856>

> [EStein said:](/goto/post?id=237852)
>
> I just ordered this test for myself. Dr Woeller is the lead doctor at Great Plains Lab. I believe you can submit your results for a small fee & he can provide an expert opinion. I see a number of areas that are of concern.
>
> Wonder what Dr Kruse's thoughts are on this test?
>
>

Thank you for posting! I just started another webinar on this test last night. The Great Plain's Website is full of great info. I think one of Jack's webinars back in 2012 or 2014 mentioned this test as being useful to get a better picture of what your dealing with when trying to reverse engineer the gut. There are so many places to start but I know everything comes back to the gut.. and by fixing this first maybe I'll fix my hormones.

I'm trying to concoct a solid plan of action based on my results.. **not 100% sure about how to tackle the clostridium..** not too keen on antibiotics, considering they don't do much for the spores. Attempts previously made were halted due to my nnEMF exposure but I've since been done my best trying to mitigate them. The only drawback is my day job at a medical office where I'm all day on the computer. I have no idea what the magnetic/electric field levels are but the RF are moderate, not the worst but certainly not the best.

At this point I'm wondering how much progress can be made on my gut because of my environment. It's certainly reduced from where I started and I do see improvements in myself since making changes, but I'm wary about it because it's not perfect yet...

As far as the test, the yeast is obviously a huge factor. I don't think I have systemic candida, at least not thrush or vaginally and I can tolerate a lot of probiotics, but I haven't come across any one botanical that seemed to hep 100%.

The Mitochondrial RX also seems appropriate at this point. I'm thinking of colloidal silver as a form of treatment but need to dig in a bit deeper yet.

I like your idea of submitting results for a fee. Probably worthwhile considering I don't have a doctor yet and I'm just picking this stuff apart myself.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-237857>

I haven't been able to order any testing for deuterium levels in my body yet.. I'm on an extremely limited budget... but I'm assuming just ordering some DDW like Qlarivia would be a worthwhile form of treatment? Sucks not to know your baseline, but a 25 or 50 ppm would be helpful regardless.

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=237745)

Hi JanSz, sorry for the late reply. Right after submitting the sample I ordered some high dose Bifido probiotic powder which included strains to break down oxalates. I also had an extra bottle of ALTA ozonated caster and ozone liquid from a while back that I started taking 3x a day along with Kirkmans biofilm defense. I started to feel a bit crappy on it .. perhaps the dose was high but I thought it was working for the yeasts. I read that Biocidin is useful for Clostridia or Colloidal Silver. After I finished the ALTA I planned to add that or just Cistus Tea/Stevia for a few months. As of yesterday I had a bout of pain and not sure the actual cause. I've stopped everything I was taking until the pain subsides. I thought I was making progress up until this point.

My gallbladder dead smack in the center of my chest and my mid back is on fire. I ended up in the ER from high fever starting around 102 then ending at 99, mildly tachycardic at rest. They found no gallstones, but "sludge." The consideration is perhaps "acalculus cholecystitis" because the wall is 6mm in size. I have a HIDA scan for next week to assess function. Though my fever has broke I still have a lot of pressure and pain, worse while standing, walking and sitting and a INTENSE BURN in my mid back directly behind the gallbladder. The pain there is worse than the gallbladder itself.

It's all very interesting because I've never had gallbladder pain before, indigestion or acid reflux. Mainly just lower bowel IBS/constipation. I'm hoping the swelling goes down soon.

### Alison N — 2018-06-11T23:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-243210>

I've been consistently on Lugols 5% (50-60mg) since January. First thyroid test on 50 mg of Iodine was in mid January of this year:

TSH was over 5 and antibodies were low (1-3 respectively).

Not sure what caused the TSH to drop and the antibodies to increase... too much Iodine? My doctor recommended I stop all together. Perhaps it's time to ease my way into eating more iodine rich foods and become less reliant on Lugols? Does anyone else stay on Lugols indefinitely? I'm worried about fatigue/brain fog coming back. Iodine is my AM wake up call.

Seem to have high Androgens. Low in E2, P4, Glutathione and all neurotransmitters. I'm watching the DUTCH tutorials to learn more.. still not an expert in understanding the results yet.

Today is menstrual day 49 and I have had no menstrual cycle as of yet.. which is VERY new to me. Not sure what the trigger is.

My cycle has been very consistent for the past 10-15 years. Usually between 38-40 days per month. One day of pain otherwise normal. I was given 100mg RX for Prometrium BHT and a tincture of Licorice, Peony and Rhodiola.

She also recommended 20,000mg of Vitamin A - which I don't think is a good idea.
She also recommended LDN - which I'm not sure I need.

Overall surprised and confused by my results.

The only other time I had thyroid antibodies was maybe around 17-20 years old from eating dairy, grains and other nasties. Once I cut that out, antibodies were consistently now. This is the first, at least documented time, that my TSH is so low/antibodies are high.

### JanSz — 2018-06-12T04:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-243228>

31 years old. I have lived in Portland, Oregon for the past 5 years. My husband and I moved from the MD & DE area near Ocean City, MD where we lived for 3-4 years prior.

Hereditary Hematomachrosis (1 allele)

I have had a low thyroid since 2003-2004 but never took medication for it. Hashimoto’s with antibodies around 33 (resolved autoimmunity with diet)

I also had two other car accidents that caused cervical stiffening and shoulder pain. A brain MRI in 2009 showed a 0.3cm “white matter lesion.”

Then chronic fatigue set in, had all the **positive mold labs** including the HLA-mold-susceptible genotype. Also **positive for Lyme (2015)**. I moved in 2016 from one very bad apartment (within 15 minutes I would be completely stupefied in that environment) to a new one.

Numbness and tingling in hands and feet. I went to the ER in November 2017 for an episode of total left hand to elbow numbness but the EKG was normal (did not run labs) and the doc mentioned it **sounded like MS to him.**

· IBS – had constipation forever, which after introducing Iodine caused tremendous loose stools, which has since stabilized between the two.

Cold hands and feet.

Body voltage measurements in the bedroom is .887 – 1.33 and floats around 1.02 with the bed pulled from the wall.

Constantly craving sea salt.

Constantly craving nuts and nut butters.

Always thirsty despite drinking 65-80 oz. of filtered water a day.

Allergies: Gold, Shrimp (acute allergy), Chicken eggs (duck eggs are good), Wheat

TSH: 5.3
FT4: 1.0
FT3: 2.0

\=======================================================================
test 6/4/2018
freeT3=3.9(2-4.4) good, make sure it stays like this or goes little up

\---------------
test 6/6/2018
cholesterol total=183 , marginally ok, should be higher
HgA1c=4.7 little low
Vit D=48.1(30-100) ng/dL low get it over 100

Thyroglobulin Antibody=6.4 (0-0.9) high

\-----------

\----------

\-----------

\===============================

You have not posted all pages of DUTCH report.
Please post complete pdf file.
Preferably attach that original file to post

\===================================

Consider getting checked and treated
for Lyme, mold
possibly MS

Get fatty acids analysis and follow up with optimization
initially at LabCorp
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
latter at
Kennedy Krieger Institute
[https://www.kennedykrieger.org/](https://www.kennedykrieger.org/)

Get super heavy duty sun exposure until you raise sufficiently yout low vit D.

Low progesterone and estrogens likely need to be supplemented.

### Alison N — 2018-06-12T05:00:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-243230>

Thanks Jansz. I'm not supplementing with any vitamin D. The sun just started coming out here in Oregon and I spend all day in the sun. If i'm on my laptop it's outside with me. So 48 without supplementation. I'm considering adding tanning in to my regime to bump it up, at least on the rainy days here, maybe twice a week.. rather than supplementing.

Also lowered the electric fields. Body voltage in bedroom where I sleep is .5 volt - .8. In living room my exposure is .6 and when I'm spending most of the time outdoors on the balcony its .2-.3 (lowest of this crappy 3rd floor apartment). No WIFI, just neighbors ambient levels.

I was given Prometrium 100mg capsules but no Estrogen supplementation. I will start that tonight. I feel pretty zombie-ish so I hope that helps.

I was advised to eat more salt in the AM with more electrolytes. Blood pressure is 120/78 with a 20 point drop after standing... I just started feeling like I could exercise more, so surprised to see cortisol so low.

Will halt on iodine for now, take the prometrium, max sun exposure/tanning.

### JanSz — 2018-06-12T16:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-243252>

**positive mold labs**
**positive for Lyme (2015)**
**sounded like MS to him

DDW
\----------------------------------------
**
Work on all above.
You may need a month or two of doxycycline to work on Lyme
that is not going to help your gut problems, but need to be done
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You should use DDW.
The least expensive would be Qlarivia.
**For at least a year RO or tap water mixed half and half with Qlarivia.
That should result in DDW-87.5ppm (D/H)
Drink only DDW.
You may want to do your soups using DDW.
When boiling soup, make it dense at first using RO or tap water on end dilute it half/half with straight Qlarivia.
When making coffee or tea, bring you water to boiling and then sto heating.
Vapors are made of light water (that you do not want to waste).**

If you want, if you have big enough freezer, you may want to prepare your RO or tap water by freezing.
**Freeze water in this special way.
RO or tap water is a mixture of light (H2O) and (tiny bit of heavy (D2O or HDO)**
RO or tap water freeze at (32°F)=0°C
Heavy water freeze at (39°F)=3.8°C
Heavy water when it is only heavy water, freezes before "normal" water freezes.
That is not the case with mixture of light + heavy.

When water first reaches freezing temperatures it is still liquid.
**More energy have to be removed for the water to start freezing.**
There is very little heavy water in tap water, when your RO or tap water reaches 32F it is still liquid.

1\. find out how long it takes for your room temperature water when is put in freezer, to reach near 32F so you do not waste much time on latter trials.

2\. Take bottle out of freezer when it is still liquid but near or at 32F
3\. shake it vigorously (ie add work=energy) to it,
the water will start freezing
**heavy water will start freezing first** the rest remains liquid
4\. pour your water (thru sieve) to another bottle of same size.
5\. put that water back into freezer for 15-30 minutes
6\. repeat shaking and draining.

Above should remove some D, deuterium from your RO or tap water without you loosing much of actual water.
How much I do not know. (But [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) advises freezing, but have not given details of how he goes about freezing his water).

I got back from a 5 day trip from Jamaica Saturday night and Mon-Today, legs swelled up. Had almond butter and coconut oil prior to my bath, after I cooked kale and bacon with spirulina. Took one bite of bacon and sat down. Immediately felt like I'd black out, heart started beating HARD and LOUD. I drove a mile down the road to the ER. ATRIAL RATE 115 BPM BPM

QRS DURATION 74 ms ms
Q-T INTERVAL 324 ms ms
Q-T INTERVAL (CORRECTED) 448 ms ms
P WAVE AXIS 67 degrees degrees
QRS AXIS 62 degrees degrees
T AXIS 44 degrees degrees
INTERPRETATION TEXT Sinus tachycardia with occasional Premature ventricular complexes
Nonspecific ST abnormality
Abnormal ECG
When compared with ECG of 27-SEP-2017 20:22,
Premature ventricular complexes are now Present
Vent. rate has increased BY 42 BPM
Confirmed by Goodwin MD, Daniel (2604) on 4/25/2019 8:59:04 PM

### Alison N — 2019-10-30T22:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-281598>

New house, some new symptoms.. weak, heavy & slightly numb right arm and about 15 minutes of dizziness and inability to explain some things with speech.. like I couldn't find works to talk. ### Alison N — 2020-04-22T01:31:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-288900>

It's been a while since my last post.

I finally moved out of an apartment and have a house. Pro's and con's still to work on.

I just purchased a 20 gauss magnetico and have been on it for two days and installed a bed canopy of swiss shield naturelle & ultima fabric. Question I can't seem to find.... I'm using the Acousticom 2 RF meter and while under the canopy the levels go undetectable, there are brief moments where the LEDs will pop up, (still very low, like .01-.3 microwats per cm^2) but are they actually pin-balling around under there causing me more harm than not? Or is that more of a worry?........ Ultimately I started to get headaches day 1 under it, day 2 I woke with a scintillating scotoma migrane... day 4, 5, 6 .. headaches but I also have extreme shoulder/neck pain that I'm trying to unravel. Its been one day on 500 mg of DMSA. I'm trying to increase DHA this week as my intake was around 3x meals a week.

Lots of ringing in the ears. It's always an uphill battle.. some good things have changed and some bad. Trying to find the sweet spot lately.

### Inger — 2020-04-22T09:49:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-288909>

Alison, you got headaches and migraine after starting to sleep in the canopy? Hmm. That does not sound so good. Can you try remove it for a couple nights and see if there is a difference? Ig headaches and tinnitus goes away, it might be the canopy is making something worse.

I know some people have had bad experience and got worse using it, when someone got better. It seems to be a very complicated thing.

### Alison N — 2020-04-22T22:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-2018.20768/post-288935>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=288909)
>
> Alison, you got headaches and migraine after starting to sleep in the canopy? Hmm. That does not sound so good. Can you try remove it for a couple nights and see if there is a difference? Ig headaches and tinnitus goes away, it might be the canopy is making something worse.
>
> I know some people have had bad experience and got worse using it, when someone got better. It seems to be a very complicated thing.

Hey Inger, well.. that is my suspicion on things, but it's also a possibility that I hurt myself installing it and carrying up four pieces of 99 lbs of magnetics myself. I spent most of the day yesterday tending my arm, back and shoulders and today I did not have a headache when I woke up. Just moving my head forward and down caused an intense pain behind my shoulders so this seems like a likely candidate but the migraine was unique.. It's not typical for headaches to occur for me.

I also added a second layer of fabric on top which seemed to shield a bit more than the first, so this makes me hopeful. I do have a smart meter I've been working to relocate off. It's proving very difficult. I've also tried many of the smart shield covers which don't do anything. Fortunately the pulses aren't crazy high, they fall into the low to moderate levels throughout the house but I know it's still bad. I'll continue to monitor for now

# Thread: heavy bleeding in perimenopause- help!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-01-27T16:56:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-233625>

I have had heavy menstrual bleeding leading at times to iron deficiency anemia since age 42 (I am now 52 yo). i have controlled it with taking supplemental iron at times and taking aleve to cut down on heavy flow about 3 times a month. now that i am closer to menopause, i am having irregular periods and bleeding for like 2 weeks straight. i am going to make an appointment with my ob/gyn as soon as possible. i know what she will do- she knows i don't want the usual remedies like an uterine ablation/mirena iud/hysterectomy, so she will give me a hormone pill, which i also don't like. but since i am either on my way or already in an anemic state again, i will take the pill unless anyone has other suggestions. i don't want to take aleve every day as that has its own risks. searching the forums, all i found was one post that said:
"N-acetylcysteine
Calcium D-Glucarate
Pregnenolone 50 mg
These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)."

### Penny — 2018-04-20T22:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-239581>

> [taiyang said:](/goto/post?id=233625)
>
> I have had heavy menstrual bleeding leading at times to iron deficiency anemia since age 42 (I am now 52 yo). i have controlled it with taking supplemental iron at times and taking aleve to cut down on heavy flow about 3 times a month. now that i am closer to menopause, i am having irregular periods and bleeding for like 2 weeks straight. i am going to make an appointment with my ob/gyn as soon as possible. i know what she will do- she knows i don't want the usual remedies like an uterine ablation/mirena iud/hysterectomy, so she will give me a hormone pill, which i also don't like. but since i am either on my way or already in an anemic state again, i will take the pill unless anyone has other suggestions. i don't want to take aleve every day as that has its own risks. searching the forums, all i found was one post that said:
> "N-acetylcysteine
> Calcium D-Glucarate
> Pregnenolone 50 mg
> These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)."

I used to bleed like a stuck pig... I mean it was ridiculous - so I went to the OB/GYN for what you are describing and while there, they took an x-ray - the x-ray teck uttered those magic words "I have no attachment to my uteris" and there it was... all of a sudden I had no attachment to mine either... so I asked the doctor if she could take it out - she said yes - then I asked her if she could take it out by Friday because my insurance was about to end - she checked, there was an operating room on Wednesday - I checked in, she took out just the uteris and left the ovaries - keep your ovaries - I brought Harry Potter book 7 to the hospital, I read it and walked out the next day - I don't even think that was legal - it became my new religion - it was the most freeing thing in the world for me - I'm a swimmer so you can imagine what a pain in the ass it was - I don't know where you live or how your health is, but I seem to do fine having surgery - recently I read the Cortisol post and the DHEA post and it was another eureka moment for me - I started experimenting with DHEA vaginally - I just use this expired DHEA PLUS from Life-flo and it has been amazing - I mean brain fog gone - so much more energy - as for sleep, I use a dab of progestrone cream - I don't even think it matters which one you use - all of a sudden I can remember my dreams - sleep is so much better - by the time you're my age (59), I don't think we're making anything - if you put the DHEA in your vagina rather than on your wrists etc, it will make however much of estrogen/testosterone you need - you can do between 3mg and 12 and it seems like you get the same result - there is a youtube video from I think it's ZRT labs where they chat about it - I also just bought some pregnenolone - but they give you 50 mg - I have read the dose should be like 2 mg and you should only do it for 5 days straight before taking a "hormone holiday" - Ray Sahelian writes a lot about it - I'm going to have lots of fun trying to not overdo it on a 50mg capsule - the other thing that has been very cool lately is salt - in fact, I'm hacking an Ayuvardic black salt at the moment that has sulfur in it - needless to say, it's a tough hack...  Just my 2 cents - I also put the DHEA cream on my face - it's all in the blogs - just read one a day -

### taiyang — 2018-04-21T00:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/heavy-bleeding-in-perimenopause-help.20769/post-239607>

thanks for writing penny, i appreciate it.

My fault. I messed up the link.

"SNP's and SAP's aint what you think" comes from here and refers to the Dec 17 webinar [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/biggest-nnemf-risk.20500/#post-228963](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/biggest-nnemf-risk.20500/#post-228963)

Between that webinar and the April 2016 webinar there is so much spoken as well as so much between the lines that they are treasure troves and I listen regularly. Out of respect for the work that was put into those webinars I'm practicing the first rule of Fight Club.

As a thought experiment (And because one of these was just staring at me for minutes and kinda Donnie Darko creeping me out a bit) say I gave you a $1M budget to raise Jackrabbits (once established you get to keep the rest of the money):
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Also say I started to try raising them in Siberia which is where you have to start from.

You could spend hundreds of thousands (chipping away your profits) coming a the smart sounding conclusion that there is a genetic "defect" in many of the rabbits that happen to have the largest heat irradiating ears. Maybe certain circulation controlling supplements and drugs could even tilt the scales a bit for some of the rabbits.

OR

You could tell me that I just chose the wrong environment. You could relocate the experiment and these little critters would multiply like rabbits. It'd be easy money in the right environment.

So, what does that meaning the end? By and large is a genetic "defect" really a defect for those rabbits just based on the fact that I threw a flag in the ground in one arbitrary place?

# Thread: Structuring The DDW
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdamF — 2018-01-28T00:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-233649>

I have some DDW coming from Dr. Boros' clinic --

Are there some simple ways to structure and enhance the DDW?

Out in the sun with a Tibetan singing bowl to carry to blast it with sound waves and then to a dark steel container for the rest of the day?

### Allin — 2018-01-28T00:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-233650>

On the bottles of DDW you will receive, it states store away from direct sunlight. I do have the other Preventa DDW bottles (which are plastic), not in the sun. I have put some of this DDW into a glass VOSS bottle, put in the frig to get cold and then drink the DDW water while outside getting sun and yes the glass bottle did experience some direct sunlight. I drink some cold DDW and then jump in the cold pool for 40 minutes, then get into the sun for an hour while drinking some more cold DDW. That is pretty much how I structure my DDW.

### TANN — 2018-07-19T18:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246204>

> [Allin said:](/goto/post?id=233650)
>
> On the bottles of DDW you will receive, it states store away from direct sunlight. I do have the other Preventa DDW bottles (which are plastic), not in the sun. I have put some of this DDW into a glass VOSS bottle, put in the frig to get cold and then drink the DDW water while outside getting sun and yes the glass bottle did experience some direct sunlight. I drink some cold DDW and then jump in the cold pool for 40 minutes, then get into the sun for an hour while drinking some more cold DDW. That is pretty much how I structure my DDW.

Hi Allin,
long time since we talked and i recall you were very helpful when i first joined the forum.
I trust you are doing well? I am very interested to know how you feel about the DDW and wondered how much you rate its performance etc?
Best Regards
TANN

### Allin — 2018-07-19T19:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246209>

> [TANN said:](/goto/post?id=246204)
>
> Hi Allin,
> long time since we talked and i recall you were very helpful when i first joined the forum.
> I trust you are doing well? I am very interested to know how you feel about the DDW and wondered how much you rate its performance etc?
> Best Regards
> TANN

_Hey Tann, Good hearing from you. I would rate DDW a must in todays world. I would like to see your levels close to or below 130 ppm of Deuterium. Mine was 147 ppm. I drank the DDW, along with spring water for 4 months and my level dropped to 130 ppm. I had increased mental clarity, more energy and less joint pain. DDW is not a magic pill but a tool to help you on the road to optimal. Sunrise sun and grounding, more sun in morning, blue blockers at night, etc are also needed. The BIG issue I have is population density and I am looking to move to east Texas. For example; a couple years ago I was in Studio City and out in the sun and had a great tan and my Vitamin D level was 19. I said what?...19...Now it is in the low 30s but I doubt if I will ever seen 40 or 50 here in West LA._

### TANN — 2018-07-24T08:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structuring-the-ddw.20772/post-246562>

Good to hear from you Allin. Its very expensive so i dont think im going to be buying the DDW. It sounds very impressive what you achieved but its a shame that levels would raise again so quickly without continued use. I know people talk about re-freezing water as well but it seems that it doesnt achieve a considerable drop in dueterium.
All the best with the move to East Texas!
TANN

# Thread: The Human Charger
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-charger.20773/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Polito — 2018-01-28T15:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-charger.20773/post-233668>

This may have been brought up in a prior thread, but I came a cross an ad about the HumanCharger. UV free full spectrum light to give the body a boost. Does anyone have any experience with it? [www.HumanCharger.com](http://www.HumanCharger.com)

### Sheddie — 2018-01-28T16:16:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-charger.20773/post-233676>

IF this passes muster, I'd have to be seriously intrigued since having implanted UV-blocking intra-ocular lenses (IOLs) after cataract extraction just 4 months before I discovered this site and began my journey with the 'new paradigm!' (Talk about 'timing!')

One of the first articles I found after becoming introduced to _Light, Water & nnEMFs_ indicated there is some clinical research on blue-blocking IOLs for older patients, especially when autoimmune conditions are a factor. (Too bad my, otherwise world-class, eye surgeon didn't 'see' that when he saw my medical records! I wrote him about it but got no response. I can only hope he considers this for future cataract patients.)

This quote is found on the How It Works page: Notice the light is 'UV-free.'

Start your bright light therapy

Simply push the power button and the UV-free, calibrated white light will start flowing through your ear canals to the light-sensitive regions of your brain.

I went to the Human Charger research site but I don't have the background to determine if the cited publications/references match to support the marketing claims. Thanks for bringing this up, Michael. I look forward to more consideration on this device.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-11-30T22:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-educate-others-on-the-effects-of-nnemf-ridiculous-responses.20777/post-257049>

Absolutely right about 5G and all other RF microwave radiation are not ionizing radiation, like X-rays, for example. Fundamental science fact.

Wireless industry (and government regulators with an incestuous relationship with them) hold as a fundamental tenant of the long presumed safety of microwaves it that it is non-ionizing and therefore can not harm or disrupt the body (as long as the exposure is under the thermal effect limit-- i.e. if it is not powerful enough to heat body tissue like a microwave oven).

We know very well now that routine low-level exposure to microwaves has a host of body-disruptive and disease causing effects-- a large number of legitimate scientific studies have demonstrated this without a shadow of doubt.

As for proselytizing 'non-believers' to alert them to RF-EMF hazards-- I regard it as essentially a waste of time.

Your time and energy is better spent protecting yourself and your loved ones-- they are the ones that really count in your individual universe.

We can't change the world but we can be an example to others in our actions. If others see this and their intellect and hearts are evolved enough to see truth in what we do, then they will change of their own volition.

# Thread: Doubly labeled water test to determine Energy Expenditure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2018-01-29T22:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233764>

A friend asked me a question today and I did not know the answer. He asked what the latest story about what Deuterium really does, means for the Doubly labeled water test to determine energy expenditure. I already said that heteroplasmy/pseudohypoxia would come into it,that outdoor versus indoor lifestyle skews the picture ,too. How much does it compromise the validity of such studies?

_"The basis of the DLW method is to follow the decline in enrichment of the stable isotopes of oxygen (oxygen-18, 18O) and hydrogen (deuterium, 2H) in body water after initial labeling of the body water pool. As oxygen-18 is lost from the body in the form of water and carbon dioxide (CO2), whereas deuterium is lost only as water, the difference in loss from the body reflects the CO2 production during the period. Because CO2 production is the result of fat, carbohydrate and protein oxidation, it is an index of energy expenditure.""

"The principle of the doubly-labeled water technique rests on indirect calorimetry; total energy expenditure is measured from the turnover rate of two stable isotopes, deuterium (2H) and oxygen 18 (18O) (Ritz & Coward, 1995). "

"In particular, energy needs in obese persons have been abundantly studied. The data obtained with the doubly-labeled water method have been compared to declared intakes obtained in dietary surveys. This comparison has revealed important discrepancies in obese as well as in lean individuals. Nowadays, data from doubly-labeled water studies are often used as standards for validating results obtained from dietary surveys. Doubly-labeled water data also serve as a basis for building equations that define "under-reporting" in dietary surveys (Black, 1996): when a subject declares energy intake that is insufficient to cover energy expenditures as measured by the doubly-labeled water method for subjects of the same gender, age and body size, then the subject is suspected of under-reporting his/her intake so much as to make it invalid."
_

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-29T23:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233767>

Many assumptions are made here. Number one being that the relationship of 0-18 acts the same way in the living system as it does in the environment. It turns out this is not true because of how the KIE shows differential effects in sweating. Therefore those DLW have come under some fire. The DLW method was shown to be accurate compared to indirect calorimetry (Westerterp et al., 1988). The method was thought to be validated in soldiers under various conditions (DeLany et al., 1989; Forbes-Ewan et al., 1989; Hoyt et al., 1991, 1994) but none of these studies controlled for the the effect of sweating as a parasympathetic controller and how deuterium maybe concentrated in tissues as light hydrogen in water its lost in sweat evaporation. The water losses in breathing also were not taken into account. What we do know is that in the Earth's environment, and especially the oceans that 0-18 and deuterium seem to have a rather liner relationship. What we know about deuterium and mitochondrial dynamics is that deuterium is not favored inside the matrix. This has left doubly labeled water theory with many problems since the early 1990's. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK233774/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK233774/)

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-29T23:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233768>

DLW has always assumed deuterium only leaves via water. We now know it leaves via the bile and it can be shed in keratin from the skin too............once you find more places it leaves your isotope studies using DLW go out the window.

### Lahelada — 2018-01-29T23:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233771>

Thank you Dr K. for taking the time to explain !! That last point about it being shed in keratin is way interesting.

### Inger — 2018-01-30T10:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233797>

Lahelada, maybe that is one reason why dry brushing does so good

### Lahelada — 2018-01-30T12:12:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-233799>

I was thinking about that ,too. Also makes me wonder about "naturally" thin or sparse and thinning hair in age.

### Lahelada — 2018-02-05T00:11:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doubly-labeled-water-test-to-determine-energy-expenditure.20778/post-234142>

Funny, until a few days ago I did not even know about doubly labeled water and now it crops up in the article you quoted. [https://www.8weeksout.com/2017/11/21/recovery-driven-fitness/](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.8weeksout.com%2F2017%2F11%2F21%2Frecovery-driven-fitness%2F&h=ATNHRfv3VHXsFDIotiSjwdQ-6lp2t53svHrFiiQNHZ9zVNUM3bcTUgXnSvyFM9mI3fa-shAL45xDNkuvuOSOLlF3dmtOV3flqmOQxi1JiGgOHvKz0r5_8uGuSV7B6kg7WBIxzlOpqCvCGnMr) There is however a doubt in my mind. Are DLW studies a true measure of metabolic potential / expenditure if deuterium is used for labeling ? In the study quoted by the article DLW is used in the African traditionally living "control group". Did they then not also "handicap" their system by X % by introducing Deuterium ? Or is the amount negligible ? In this case it could also be offset by the stronger equatorial sun . However most metabolic studies do not have sunlight as a mitigating factor. My question really is whether a) metabolic studies based on DLW can be reliable and b whether maybe they can even influence the health of the individuals either temporarily or permanently depending on the length of the study and the state of the individual in the first place. [http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(15)01577-8](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822\(15\)01577-8)

I think "bad" sex is ubiquitous, and can happen even in "good" long term relationships. I honestly couldn't even imagine meeting a woman who has not had bad sex. I think the author of this article sums it up nicely. I was perplexed about the #metoo movement in this respect as well--what woman has lived without any sexual harassment or misogynistic marginalization? We aren't taught as women to honor our own bodies or needs or desires, the concepts around menstruation are shrouded in shame and confusion, pregnancy is seen as a medical event that we are best left to let someone else manage for us, bodies are hyper-sexualized, everyday language is filled with ways that we oppress the feminine--"don't be a pussy," "don't be hysterical." I agree with the author here we are conditioned to believe that being female means accepting pain (that is, in fact, historically how modern day obstetrics got on a roll--things like "twilight sleep" were seen as a way for a woman to empower herself to establish control over her "inevitable" pain in labor). On another level the concepts and strengths seen as feminine, and that come from the yin energy are similarly looked down upon--the ability to wait something out without taking action in the face of adversity (the art of nondoing), service and nurturing jobs are typically paid less--caregivers and teachers. These kinds of things are entrenched in our world economy, brilliantly elucidated by minds like Marilyn Waring, who brought awareness to the global stage that economies are built on destruction and war, and no economic value is placed on things like preserving our natural lands or clean water, or on the invisible efforts of women who stay at home with children thereby enabling their partners to work. Bad sex is going to happen until women are able to remember and realize their sacredness. The shift is happening.

### caroline — 2018-02-05T07:02:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-sex-is-bad.20780/post-234152>

I have shared a lot of things here because I think ultimately everyone benefits ......but this is a little too personal - altho I think I have talked to you Dan about this privately.

In the long run it was a simple issue to do with menopause and inactivity because my DH had been so sick for so many years.

At the end of the day - It really was so simple and a Doc should have picked it up pretty fast. I wound up listening to my own intuition and ...I pretty much figured it out for myself with the help of Susan Wright in the U.S. who is a nurse practitioner.

One female Doc in her late thirties thought I was crazy to be asking about BHRT - she said she would never do anything that stupid. Another female Doc who specialises in female vaginal issues was no help at all. Her manner was totally dismissive. I would have crumbled and given up if I hadn't know better because of jackkruse.com.

I have so much to be grateful for ........

### Mayuri — 2018-02-05T10:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-sex-is-bad.20780/post-234157>

Well, so much to consider here. My experience is that we are all so blooming conditioned. Hollywood dictates what kind of sex we should be having. We see female orgasms being performed on TV and then men expect that and same with their performances. The huff and puff breathing and then the moaning; and give me a break! My male friend said to me, "my partner told me she has orgasms every time we make love. Do you think she does?"
I just gave him that Gregory Peck look, with one eye brow up. Honestly, not all women have orgasms as they do in the movies! Sometimes its very subtle, other times it can generate endorphins and its a nice high, it doesn't always have to be a heavy pulsating and yet we're all conditioned to expect the big bang. It's sad really. Albeit, there are different kinds of orgasms, clitoral and uterine, and every woman is different. Yes, I think women are often under pressure to deliver the performance. The truth is that some need heavy stimulation and others very light stimulation, it's not all that h o l l y w o o d nonsense. Women are under pressure to perform according to the 'script', and I think men are too. Have to say I don't know too many women who have pain during sex. I didn't and don't. The article says only 30%. Perhaps older women are more prone to pain? For certain, foreplay is vitally important for women to have orgasms. That means taking one's time during sex and these days not too many have the time. We're exhausted from work and over stressed. Additionally, people really need sex nowadays because of the severe stress everyone is under. Somehow it's not about love anymore.
The old adage that you can tell when someone hasn't had sex is true, people get very uptight and some even go mad - for instance; when their partner is impotent, there are actual psychological difficulties.
So, trying to answer the question here: I think if you can design something to make sex more playful and relaxed for both parties, to somehow make pleasure easier to reach - why not?
Staged sex = Bad sex

I was issued with a handbag and a shorter raincoat than the men...i was given one pair of trousers and 6 pairs of collottes (skirt with seam) even though i lived in a place that was regularly minus 10 over winter....i suffered relentless sexual harassment from colleagues and bosses for my entire career (23 years)....i was treated like a handicap on on my squads at work... women were rationed and considered a handicap... my expertise and experience as a police officer was very often undermined and I was treated more like a cheer leader than a team leader...things changed a lot over that time but I think I suffered some of the worse times in law enforcement as a woman...my work and experience was constantly under valued compared to men who were junior to me....i was told that i could not join the dog squad or the mounted police because my periods would upset the animals. I could not apply for deployment to an island because it had a high rate of domestic violence and I was likely to get raped. These were official reasons provided to me. ### drezy — 2018-02-06T03:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-sex-is-bad.20780/post-234206>

> [Mayuri said:](/goto/post?id=234203)
>
> What I mean by staged is: somehow we've been given a check list.
> Do this, must do that first, then that, definitely must do this, or, he /she think that I stink in bed. That kind of staged. Why not before you do anything talk it over?
> The license to discuss sex and what he or she likes has been eradicated, withdrawn - in many instances it's a performance. Where do these staged 'steps' come from? They've been given to us; conditioning.
> Women should talk about what they like, and often they just don't. Often they don't know.
>
> One woman whose partner was impotent from medication thought my suggestion of discussing with her husband what he could do to please her was outrageous.
> She was a young woman in her 40's. I said to her 'your husband would love it if you did that. He must feel terrible to place you in the position of not having sex."
> She wouldn't do it and she's very unwell now and on medication herself now. Horrors!
>
> Why not find out what each person likes, discuss and explore - it makes for better and genuine intimacy.
> Now sure I know, we all know, often sex is spontaneous and happens in heated passion - we feel the fire and actions can come naturally.
> But when one is with a partner for awhile this spontaneity can wane. Then sex can become, got to do this, got to do that and like this and like that. This is what I mean by staged.
>
> I remember when I was a young girl, I happened upon my brother reading a book about how to turn on a woman.
> He said, 'oh tickling the inner thigh is supposed to work great. '
> He came home after spending time with his partner and said, "it works." Also, he reported most of the things in the book, didn't work. He had to find out directly from his partner what she liked.
> It was funny really, because I learned this erogenous zone from my brother and to this day, decades later, it makes me smile that I got the tip from him. This was before sex scenes in the movies and on cable series.
>
> I remember having a partner who came to me with this idea and that idea, I said, "where on earth did you learn that idea."
> Truly, I didn't wish for beads to go up my bum, nor did I wish to try it. But guess what? Some do! He sure sounded like he wanted to try it out on himself (stop laughing) but I wasn't into it. Hey, whatever works for you.
>
> I think it admirable for a man to want to please a woman and vis-a-versa but not everyone is the same.
> Some friends will share what they did with a new boyfriend and I'll say, "You did what? Why? You hardly know him. I'll ask "Where did you get that idea from? " HOLLYWOOD S H I T. "Did he call you again?" "No". It's so sad, and it's so upsetting that we are strangers to ourselves.
>
> Let's ask ourselves, how do we all have sex without Dopamine? Perhaps that is why we take up that 'script'? Because the majority of us are operating on empty. Dr. Jack is saving us all from endless horrors.
>
> Women need to take back control of what they think is expected from and of them.
>
> Everyone has different erogenous zones and I think that's where we need to go to. . . to personalised preferences through discussions and explorations. And of course, we all need to establish Dopa-Dopa-Dopamine practices in a big way. It's obvious, behaviour is very un-natural these days. We have Dr. Jack to thank for opening our eyes.
