Hi Sue,
It's great to hear from someone else dealing with our UK environment. ;-)

Have you been completely dark for those 10-12 hours or do you have firelight? We have only used candles and fire once it has gone dark for a few months now, but as this is the only time we get to study, cos of work, we are usually listening to Jack or watching him on youtube (blueblockers on of course), so I guess this isn't completely dark...
I haven't come across a migraine cup - I will have to explore that. We have been cold showering in the morning and before bed. I do find the cold a great hack for better sleep quality. We are dealing with a high level of stress at the moment and we are still sleeping - and we are really pleased, as our sleep has often suffered at stressful times of our lives in the past.
I had forgotten that hack with the bed head. We were doing this in our old house, but we moved a couple of years ago and we haven't raised our bed head back up yet. And we had made our own grounding sheet with a cable out the window onto a grounding rod - but we haven't re-instated that at our new house either. We did try a grounding sheet that plugs into the earth in the wall - but didn't like it - I am sure I could feel some sort of energy interference - maybe the earthing system in this new house is not so good? We don't use it any more. We had a talk with our neighbour here, and he said he needed to put in 5 earthing straps to earth his house electric system! We live on the side of a hill, and the ground is very rocky sandstone with thin soils - he said it was because there are so many voids in the ground that it didn't earth very well. I wondered if this could have an effect on reducing the benefits of grounding by being barefoot outside here? Have you any thoughts on that?

### Sue-UK — 2018-02-17T16:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-234979>

> [CarolandNick said:](/goto/post?id=234971)
>
> Hi Sue,
> It's great to hear from someone else dealing with our UK environment. ;-)
>
> Have you been completely dark for those 10-12 hours or do you have firelight? We have only used candles and fire once it has gone dark for a few months now, but as this is the only time we get to study, cos of work, we are usually listening to Jack or watching him on youtube (blueblockers on of course), so I guess this isn't completely dark...
> I haven't come across a migraine cup - I will have to explore that. We have been cold showering in the morning and before bed. I do find the cold a great hack for better sleep quality. We are dealing with a high level of stress at the moment and we are still sleeping - and we are really pleased, as our sleep has often suffered at stressful times of our lives in the past.
> I had forgotten that hack with the bed head. We were doing this in our old house, but we moved a couple of years ago and we haven't raised our bed head back up yet. And we had made our own grounding sheet with a cable out the window onto a grounding rod - but we haven't re-instated that at our new house either. We did try a grounding sheet that plugs into the earth in the wall - but didn't like it - I am sure I could feel some sort of energy interference - maybe the earthing system in this new house is not so good? We don't use it any more. We had a talk with our neighbour here, and he said he needed to put in 5 earthing straps to earth his house electric system! We live on the side of a hill, and the ground is very rocky sandstone with thin soils - he said it was because there are so many voids in the ground that it didn't earth very well. I wondered if this could have an effect on reducing the benefits of grounding by being barefoot outside here? Have you any thoughts on that?

Completely dark, and no heating from a fire or candles, window open to let the cold air in. It was a bit strange at first going to bed by 8 pm, with sunrise so late in the morning, but I quickly adapted. If I fall asleep soon after 8 I might wake up for a while in the night, but its a meditative, peaceful time, and I'll then go back to sleep until just before sunrise. Sometimes a dream seems to wake me up, they are more vivid and more detailed IYKWIM. I have an eye injury that means I can't read in dim light, and it was a comment by Alexander Wunsch reported by a member who had had a consultation with him, that he believed that we needed more than 8 hours darkness to regenerate that started the hack off. With the lack of sunlight over the winter, I needed to find an alternative. I got away to the sun for 10 days recently (loads of rain and high winds ....) I went because I could, and wanted to, but I wasn't _desperate_ to go like I have been in the past. I did 11-12 hours darkness at night whilst I was there because it has been such a game changer for me.

My yoga grounding mat came with a lead to go into the wall, but I paid extra for an external grounding rod. Intuitively I wouldn't use it plugged into the wall socket, and to check DH removed the live and neutral from a plug, and just from the earth pin to a grounding wire his voltmeter picked up .8 volts. When I first got it I had a couple of nights where I didn't feel it was working, and when I checked the wire had come off the mat. I also had 4 days in Amsterdam over new year (DD lives there) and with nnemf, no grounding, population density, flights etc I came back at midnight inflamed and with my muscles really sore, whinging about how crap I felt. (Although I appeared to sleep well there, it wasn't regenerative, the muscle aches and pains seemed to accumulate). Up at sunrise after even a late night with my set up and I sprung out of bed with I'd say a 95% improvement. The last little bit was where I'd twisted my knee while I was away, and that took another 24 hours to clear. My feet and calves go directly on the mat (it wipes clean easily), not under a sheet (even 100% cotton).

With outside grounding, although I know there's no underground cabling, I gravitate to certain spots more than others, get my sunrise grounding in from one spot, and different spots during the day depending on where I can get the best from the angle of the sun. When its completely overcast, or at night time is when I can gauge whether a particular spot is good for grounding, because I'm not second guessing whether its the variable of the sun that is having the positive effect. Before I got the mat I would go outside and ground before going to bed, and I'll still do that when dusk is later, and I can move around safely without artificial light.

### CarolandNick — 2018-03-02T10:47:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-236134>

Our friends and neighbours laugh at us, and cannot see why we enjoy getting outside first thing in the morning every day - whatever the weather ;-)
This morning was cold, but beautiful. My feet seem to cope with frozen ground really well (as long as I don't move around too much). I am assuming that a covering of snow can help in some way to compensate for the lower light levels, from reflecting the light back as well as the CT effects?
We are experiencing a full moon at the same time as a complete covering of snow. It is unusual for these phenomena { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

to come together for us here in our part of the UK. Last night it never seemed to be dark at all - very weird light!

### CarolandNick — 2018-05-17T12:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-241390>

I have been musing on the recent results from my HRV testing with Dr Kucera in contrast to the results I have been getting when my friend does the exact same test - but in my work environment. They are very different. At work my readings are very poor - and this has been a cause for concern that my heath is not picking up - even with all the protocols I have in place. Oddly though, the tests I have done at Dr Kucer's clinic - an hours drive from work - - show results that are greatly improved and are no real cause for concern. This really hit me last week when I took the tests only 6 or 7 days apart and the results were crazy different.
I have been exploring my nnEMF environment recently, and was shocked by how much of a "soup" I have been sitting in to work. I am in the process of changing what I can with this - but what does anyone think? Could these non-consistant readings be associated to the effects of the nnEMFs on my body? For the reaings by my friend I would stop my work at the computer and walk into her office across the hall and with the Dr I am away from work for an hour or so before he takes the reading.... Puzzled...

### CarolandNick — 2018-05-28T20:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-242212>

**Very exciting day!**
This is the first firing of our home built wood fired sauna in our garden. We have been working on this project since last autumn, so enjoying our own home sauna experience has been hugely anticipated.... and it didn't let us down! We were concerned we would not be able to heat the space enough with our stove - made with the old gas bottle stove from our yurt. But it worked a treat! We reached a high enough temperature with ease.
The sauna cabin is a repurposed summer house that we bought from ebay. We live on the side of a hill, and our garden is on 4 levels up the hillside, so even getting the shed into place was a challenge. We have to carry everything we need up to the sauna site - around 20 stone steps. Real functional fitness training ;-)
We have left the front windows in the cabin - but created some "double-glazed' units to keep in the heat. Being able to look out over nature - just the trees and hillside is ace. Maybe we will need to make some blinds as time goes on, we were thinking of natural felt maybe, for extra insulation in the winter, but for now it is fine.
We have insulated and the whole shed, tiled the floor, panelled the walls with some great quality pine, and of course installed the stove and chimney.

We have yet to build the seating etc, but our garden chairs are working fine for now ;-)
Our plan is to create two tiers of benches so we can sit at different levels. Crucially though, we plan to make the lower bench extendable to become a double bed. We really want to be able to sleep in this cabin too, sometimes - to be completely away from all our electricity. Can't wait!
We have come across various reports of the benefits of traditional Finnish sauna - using wood fires rather than electric heaters. We will be experiencing IR from the fire - but is there a study into the differences between wood fired saunas and modern infrared saunas that anyone is aware of. I would love to know more. Maybe some of the Scandinavian members could help me with this?

### CarolandNick — 2019-01-26T19:44:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-261473>

I feel so lucky that both Nick, my partner and I just love reading and researching Jack's humongous body of work. When we have free time together we love listening to a webinar or reading a blog post so we can discuss it all. We have been on this journey together for over 30 years now, and discovering Jack's work in 2017 was a real life changer.
We have worked together for most of that time running our own business selling natural foods and remedies. The way we run our business has changed several times over the years as research lead us to reconsider our approach. We both now fully understand that food isn't everything - it is our environment that is key. This has lead to some interesting conversations with our customers. It is very clear that the vast majority cannot grasp this thought though and we have to know when to stop!. We hope we are leading by example as we do tell people what we are doing. It is great when we see "light-bulb" moments in people and they start asking us more questions and wearing the glasses, getting outside etc.... We are not a conventional "health food shop" and hope our shop can act as a gateway to new ideas for people when they come in to discuss their health concerns. We do carry some of the standard stuff as we need to make a living, but we hope some good does rub off on people when we talk with them.

### CarolandNick — 2019-01-26T19:55:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-261476>

Another thing I have been thinking about today is when Nick and I met Jack at Heathrow airport last year and we were able to help him out by driving him to his destination. We spent a couple of hours with JK and looking back - what an amazing opportunity! There are now so many things I wish we had talked about and didn't. We were quite new to all this then - we still are compared to so many people here. I am hopeful that we will have other opportunities to speak with Dr K in the future.

### CarolandNick — 2019-02-10T20:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-262796>

We have just taken our Cornet meter out for a walk around where we live and have been observing our environment.

There are approx 20 homes within 1/2 a mile of us and maybe 30 within a radius of a mile - for for this small island we are in a quiet area. Our house is on the side of a hill amongst trees. When the trees have no leaves we have massive views west, north and south as we look over the Cheshire Plain. We can see hundreds of square miles. This was one of the things that drew us here. Now I have mixed feelings about this long view. At night I feel insulted by the lights I can see. I know I am even seeing lights from Liverpool Airport which must be 30-40 niles away!
We have another airport - Manchester, only 14 miles as the crow flies. I am now wondering if this is too close? It must be 5G enabled already. Most of the time the flight paths don't come our way, it depends on the wind I guess.

I find myself looking forwards to the trees having their leaves again so they block out the view and shelter us from the lights.

The hill we live on is not very high, but it is the highest point around here - 800 feet  The lane past our home doesn't go anywhere so there is not much activity here apart from mountain bikers and walkers enjoying the views. At the top of our garden we can see east as well, but that looks into a range of hills so we don't get such a long view.

We have been assessing the cell towers etc that we can see and the distances from us. Our closest big tower is about 1 mile away to the north, maybe a little more. I am not aware of any smaller cell towers closer than a mile away from our home. We have a mast that links to Manchester Airport on a hill to the south about 2 miles away. To the east there is a mast on the ridge about 5 miles away. To the west we have Jodrell Bank - a group of radio telescopes that is around 8 miles away. (Can anyone tell me what may be coming from there and if we should be concerned?)

We have noted the readings from the Cornet as we walked around the paths here, and now we need to work out what they mean.When we walked along the lane the signal was pulsing, peaking at around 7mW/m2, but when we walked along the ridge line it was more stable at around 0.05 - 0.005 mW/m2. Interestingly, the place with the lowest reading was were we sit outside the back of our house to eat our meals 0.0005mW/m2. Nice! I then took mu phone off airplane mode and the reading shot up to 105!!

Making sense of what all this means is not my strong point, and I intend to take the meter out with me more so I can get better used to interpreting what I see.

### Jack Kruse — 2019-02-10T21:41:08+0200

Interesting. Thank you for your input. We have walked around the area again, and there is no pulse where our house actually is. It is in trees, and I guess that is helping. We start to pick up that pulsing RF about 300m down the road. I used to think we were a long way from the airport... I am re-evaluating that one!
How far do the nnEMFs from airports reach? What would be called a safe distance?

### CarolandNick — 2019-03-02T21:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-264311>

When my OH was out a couple of weeks ago an old lady pulled out and smashed up our trusty Subaru. Luckily no-one was hurt - just the car! We have been driving a hire car for the last couple of weeks - new and full of tech, unlike our Forester. We have had to make some long journeys to view potential car replacements and we both noticed we slept really badly after those journeys and I had a really strong stomach ache the day following one of these journeys too - very unlike me, so I used the Cornett again to try to form a picture of what kind of fields we were sitting in. The most noticeable was a reading on the gauss meter higher than I had seen anywhere in my measuring so far. None of the readings were very good compared to our old car.
We reassessed what kind of vehical we wanted to buy - a low tech one was the priority. We have been really lucky and found just what we wanted. A 20 year old Landcruiser in amazing condition. Walnut dash, leather seats, sunroof and not much tech - it even has an analogue clock
Strange how things work out.

### CarolandNick — 2019-03-02T21:33:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-264315>

..... a lovely thing happened today at work..... we talk to people in our shop - if they will listen , and we have spent quite a time talking with a local lady who has shopped with us on and off for 20 years. She told us she has MS around a year ago, and so we have tried to offer helpful ideas in her direction. She is slowly taking things on board. Like so many people she is more open to dietary changes, but at long last we persuaded her to get some blue blockers. We sell some German ones, made by a company that Dr Wunsch advises.
She was so excited when she came in the shop today. She said from the first day she started to wear them she slept for 5 1/2 hours straight - something she hasn't done for years. Normally she is asleep for around 3 hours, then up and pacing around the house in pain, taking medication and going back to sleep for another hour or so - up again and so on. This was not a one-off either. It has happened every night for the last 5 nights!! I am so happy for her.
She is one of a small group of MS suffers that Terry Wahls is personally coaching and monitoring in an 18 month trial. They are about 3 months in. She is getting one-to-one sessions with her every week. But - do you know when these sessions are happening? In the middle of the night because of the time difference!! She was staying up until 3am on the nights of the Dr sessions. I wonder if she will be able to stay awake now she has her glasses

### CarolandNick — 2019-03-09T14:38:00+0200

You know those moments when the stars align and something you would like to happen materialises?
I had a random thought the other day to look for a second hand Magnetico, as I know we are not in the position to buy one new - and low and behold there it was..... a super sleep system at a . great price. We are going to collect it tomorrow.
I now have some questions if anyone can help?
This is 2 pads of 10 gauss each. I know I need to do some reading but the experience here is really valuable.
First, would it be better to lay them side by side under our mattress in our double bed and both sleep on them each night, or would it be a good idea to make up a single bed with both layers together at 20 gauss and then Nick and I can take turns sleeping on it? Unless we fall really lucky again  I don't expect to get another set soon.
I was listening to the Sept 2014 Q & A this morning, again just a chance choice of morning listening - and there was a discussion about the edges of the magnetico mattress being a possible issue and maybe joining them was not a good idea. I also often find myself sleeping with my feet outside the bottom of the bed over-hanging the mattress.
I am looking for things to read to get more informed - suggestions welcome!

### CarolandNick — 2019-03-11T17:57:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal-carol-and-nick.20859/post-265125>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/aldeburgh-jpeg.11096/) [@Anne V](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22299/) - Look where we ended up yesterday!!
Weird after us talking and me saying we rarely go East...
Anne, I thought the town was a great choice to live in for a while... and with fresh seafood so easily available - we had a feast ;-) It was a stormy day yesterday and the light was wonderful. I also liked the amount of water around -on 3 sides of the town - it is nearly an island. There were plenty of people out and about, lots of nice places to walk and sit, so maybe meeting new people might not to so hard.
The only thing I would want to know is if the power station in the distance has an impact?

### CarolandNick — 2019-08-04T09:48:46+0300

In the sauna last night, I was watching and wondering as the water started to appear through my skin - is there deuterium in this water? Does the sweat that we get in a sauna come from inside or outside the cell?

# Thread: Leader lesson???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leader-lesson.20860/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-12T07:23:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leader-lesson.20860/post-234596>

What do Jobs, Gates, and Putin have in common? Their kids don't use cell phones. Now we know Putin confesses to not using a smartphone - Russia Beyond, 8th February 2018
This doesn’t stop him keeping in touch with his close friends though.
“You said that everybody has a smartphone, but I don’t have one, and you don’t have one, do you? And he says that everybody has!” Russian President Vladimir Putin said while addressing the head of The Kurchatov Institute, which researches nuclear energy.
Putin was confirming what the public was told back in 2014 when his press secretary Dmitry Peskov told journalists the president uses “other means of communication...” [https://www.rbth.com/lifestyle/327542-putin-confesses-not-using-smartphone#.Wnz76vTgBkY.twitter](https://www.rbth.com/lifestyle/327542-putin-confesses-not-using-smartphone#.Wnz76vTgBkY.twitter)

# Thread: Hot versus cold water
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Technosmith — 2018-02-12T19:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/post-234622>

Hey folks,

In terms of striving for optimum and effects on EZ, how important do you guys think it is to drink your water unheated?

Certainly in a cooler climate, such as where I live in the UK, heated drinks/fluids are the norm and have been for years (as far as I'm aware).

Also, I've read some opinions that heated water may be more beneficial for healing, perhaps relating to the avoidance of the need to heat up the fluid in the body or due to it removing impurities during the heating process.

Opinions and experiences would be interesting, alongside the sciency bit of course!!

Thanks,

Phil

### brandie — 2018-02-12T19:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-versus-cold-water.20862/post-234628>

Water expands with heat.

Water shrinks with cold.

When you're talking quantum, smaller is what functions in our bodies best.

# Thread: New to this
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.20863/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### marshall mueller — 2018-02-12T23:48:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.20863/post-234640>

Greetings to all. I just learned of Jack's work and I'm completely blown away. So much here. I'm pretty clueless as to how to use a forum - so I hope I'm posting this in the right spot. Can someone tell me how to ask Jack a question?
I searched for answers but found no results.
please pardon my avatar pic. That's my granddaughter and somehow it loaded and I don't know how to fix.

### caroline — 2018-02-13T00:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.20863/post-234643>

Hi and welcome...

You can "ask Jack" if you are a silver or gold or platinum member. If you can become a gold member you will have access to all of Jack's blogs and the follow up live Q&A when you can ask Jack anything.
Jack needs us to have skin in the game!

You are in exactly the right spot!

It would be helpful if you start a journal in the optimal journal section and give Dr. K. some health history - also your Mom's and Gran's.

Tell us where you live and work....

There is a ton here! understatement of the century!

Jack's podcasts are a quick and easy way to get information to start with. Make sure to watch both of Jack's Vermont talks - they will give you a great overview of all this.

# Thread: MitoBlue
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2018-02-12T23:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234641>

I am wondering if anyone has any updates on their use of MB?

We have been using 2 drops of MB pure almost daily and have really good energy.

Does anyone use the SKQ1 from this company? the MB cream or mist?

Josh is a member here - maybe he has more updates on his products?

### Penny — 2018-02-13T02:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234650>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=234641)
>
> I am wondering if anyone has any updates on their use of MB?
>
> We have been using 2 drops of MB pure almost daily and have really good energy.
>
> Does anyone use the SKQ1 from this company? the MB cream or mist?
>
> Josh is a member here - maybe he has more updates on his products?

I ran out of Josh's stuff and went back to Kordon... My husband started to get the flu yesterday and was vomitting so much he actually let me give him MB... so I put a couple drops in a glass of ice water and a while later - an hour or so maybe - he stopped puking... he got up out of bed around 5:00 (10 hours after this started) after sleeping for a great while and eating crushed ice (another drop of MB in that) and get this - wanted to get on the exercise bike... I told him JUST NO! You need to heal not exercise... he ate some broth with lime juice and has been up and around today - no fever, nada... my neighbor also put some on her psoriasis (like it was 4 inches in diameter) and it went away - I also put some hydrogen peroxide in a steamer for him but not sure if that did anything - I do that plus a UV light when I feel like I'm getting ill and it works great though

My youngest son - regularly - uses I think too much will stay up until 4:00 A.M. because "I had so much energy" - kind of a fail in that regard - I gave my kids some Malbec and MB last night and so far none of us have the flu...

### caroline — 2018-02-13T02:26:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234651>

Malbec and mito blue - why didn't I think of that! Thanks Penny.

### brandie — 2018-02-13T02:37:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234652>

I used Kordon on eczema. It left a deepish wound that actually healed in a matter of days. Normally I used a fungicide that made eczema stop. MB seemed to 'remove" it. Interesting to me.

### caroline — 2018-02-13T03:07:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234655>

> [brandie said:](/goto/post?id=234652)
>
> I used Kordon on eczema. It left a deepish wound that actually healed in a matter of days. Normally I used a fungicide that made eczema stop. MB seemed to 'remove" it. Interesting to me.

cream? or straight up?

### brandie — 2018-02-13T04:12:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234663>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=234655)
>
> cream? or straight up?

Straight up. I dropped one drop onto small paint brush and painted it on. Quit itching almost instantly. 24 hours it was a big scab. Repainted a day or two later and used my red light canopy. Next time scab fell off there was very new skin exposed and a divot showing how deep it was, 1/4" or so. Within hours of that new skin was covering the divot.
I also use 1-2 drops in a full bath once or twice a week. Went overboard once with five drops two days in a row. Felt good but didn't feel sleepy that second night at all . Strange thing was my back, which is normally stiff, was very limber.

### caroline — 2018-02-13T04:35:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234667>

if I have MB too late I don't sleep well either........

### WalterNL — 2018-02-14T09:41:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234716>

I drink San Pellegrino with some lemon juice, ACV and a drop of MB most days. Best drink there is for my taste.
I'd make it 1/3 DDW if I had anything left.

### Brent Patrick — 2018-02-14T10:29:47+0200

I only take in the morning Caroline ,I have noticed the same late in the evening.
While waiting for sunrise I usually take MB four times a week with a dozen oysters and a mug of Beef bone broth mixed with Turmeric and a shot glass of fresh squeezed lemon juice each time.This is my Breakfast , I notice increased cognitive function within the Hour and it builds during the day way into the afternoon!

### Penny — 2018-02-14T18:13:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234746>

> [Jude said:](/goto/post?id=234713)
>
> Penny, what is Kordon? thanks.

Hi Jude Kordon is the methylene blue you get at the fish store to treat ich:
[https://www.amazon.com/Methylene-Bl...8624694&sr=8-1&keywords=Kordon+methylene+blue](https://www.amazon.com/Methylene-Blue-General-Prevention-Treatment-Aquarium/dp/B00025646W/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1518624694&sr=8-1&keywords=Kordon+methylene+blue)

Actually, I just ran a "hospital tank" with some MB for my goldfish... alas, I am overfeeding them Funny thing though - I stopped using the overhead light so much and I think the really overweight fish has vastly improved...

### Penny — 2018-02-14T18:14:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234747>

### Penny — 2018-02-14T18:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234750>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=234651)
>
> Malbec and mito blue - why didn't I think of that! Thanks Penny.

I find if I crave "something fun" after my evening meal, a glass of Malbec is fun enough Once I worked somewhere and 3 guys went to lunch and ate the same thing - 2 guys came down with dysentery - the third guy - who had 2 beers with his lunch (prime rib) was fine... so, since hubby was vomiting, I went for the wine - and gave my kids some too

I think I'll go back to one drop in the morning as I have had way too much energy at night lately...

I wondered about using it on my face to get rid of skin that wasn't turning over well in recent years. I even considered using it with my ultrasound machine, but I am chicken shit. I also wondered how long I would be stained blue.

If anyone has answers or knowledge of the things I wonder, feel free to educate me

### WalterNL — 2018-02-15T08:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234794>

### Penny — 2018-02-15T21:50:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234832>

> [brandie said:](/goto/post?id=234783)
>
> MB in your eyes? I was scared to put it directly on my skin, it turned out ok. But my eyes, whew...my five drop bath made my feet peel. I think they needed it.
>
> I wondered about using it on my face to get rid of skin that wasn't turning over well in recent years. I even considered using it with my ultrasound machine, but I am chicken shit. I also wondered how long I would be stained blue.
>
> If anyone has answers or knowledge of the things I wonder, feel free to educate me

DO NOT USE METHYLENE BLUE DIRECTLY IN YOUR EYES... I recently ran into some study/screw up where some surgeons used it on the patient's eyes instead of whatever they usually use and they went blind... having said that, I have filled a salad bowl with ice and water and stuck 1 drop of MB in it, opened my eyes during face dunks \*multiple time\* and never had a problem... so caveat emptor... I did it because JK said it would be neat to have MB eye drops... just like it's great to stick \*diluted\* H2O2 in your ears (Debrox) - probably not so hot putting it straight - I mean, get H2O2 on your fingers and the skin peels - I have used it multiple times on my skin straight with zero problem - in fact, I have some squamous/ who-knows-what on my face and it has dissipated immensely with MB - I should be sticking a 660nm light on it, but, too lazy  @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) - for cataracts, I thought the go to thing was l-carnosine eye drops from lef.org (bright eyes II) - I assumed it would help with the ubiquitination...

### caroline — 2018-02-16T07:37:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitoblue.20864/post-234872>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=234832)
>
> DO NOT USE METHYLENE BLUE DIRECTLY IN YOUR EYES... I recently ran into some study/screw up where some surgeons used it on the patient's eyes instead of whatever they usually use and they went blind... having said that, I have filled a salad bowl with ice and water and stuck 1 drop of MB in it, opened my eyes during face dunks \*multiple time\* and never had a problem... so caveat emptor... # Thread: supplements vs sun-pplements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### taiyang — 2018-02-13T00:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234644>

i am confused about jack's view on supplements. on the one hand he says to be wary of the pill pushers- ie supplement sellers and that light water magnetism is what we most need, with dha from seafood for the battery..on the other hand he sells supplements and sometimes talks about needing supplements for certain conditions.

so i am confused. for members who have been around here longer, how do you sort this apparent contradiction? right now i am supplementing with iron since i could feel my body dropping into anemia again after many weeks of bleeding due to perimenopause (i have stopped bleeding now). when i drop into anemia, my tongue gets sore. now after supplementing for a week, it's feeling more normal, so i caught it early. my regular doctor won't give me a test for ferritin because he says testing once a year is enough the dummy.

### Penny — 2018-02-13T01:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234648>

You have a low redox...
[https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/redox-rx/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-24-are-myopia-light-and-aging-linked/)
So if you read Time #21 and you wondering how ferritin works as a semiconductor you are in luck. Iron is an essential trace element that is used to form molecules in the body, such as hemoglobin. Ferritin is the major storage protein for iron. Free iron is toxic for cells because of its reactivity. So what controls its release? Its charge within the ferritin protein controls its release. Since iron is very reactive to sunlight becuase of its large amount of D shell electrons. Iron has a higher rate of emissivity than other atoms in cells and atoms with this ability readiate more energy than expected. When their emissivity is in the IR range of light, the proteins containing iron seem to be more energized. Emission of light energy reflects the intensity of charge movement in a protein. Ferritin is a solar active protein. Sunlight wavelengths can change when light travels through the atomic material in a cell or in blood. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is when sunlight energized blood plasma solar radiation drives the charge movement which generates new electromagnetic waves that can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment. So when ferritin rises it is usually because people are not spending enough time in the sun. The body up-regulates ferritin production in this case awaiting its activation. Many people think that ferritin elevation is a sign of inflamamtion but it is actually a sign that these people live a solar spectral deficient life. If you lack sunlight, you have less electrons and more protons in tissues and this lowers the pH and this decreases the exclusion zone (EZ) in water. Ferritin is the protein within the body that stores iron and releases it through channels in a controlled fashion. The unique structure of ferritin forms a spherical shell in which the iron is "stored" as Fe(III) in a crystalline mineral. Ferritin consists of 24 peptide subunits that form two types of channels where these subunits intersect; the 3-fold channel is polar and the 4-fold channel is nonpolar. The residues that line the channels determine the polarity of the channel. Polarity refers to significant differences in charges due to the electronegativity between adjacent atoms in a molecule. The polarity of the channel link to the size of the EZ in blood plasma. The iron in the ferritin core is stored as Fe(III) in a crystalline solid. This crystal acts as a semiconductor inside of ferritin. The smallest repeating unit of the ferritin lattice is called the unit cell. The unit cell for iron is ferrihydrite. In the mineral ferrihydrite, every Fe(III) ion is coordinated to six O(II) ions. Oxygen is very electronegative compared to Fe(III). However, in ferritin, the mineral core has approximately 10% of the Fe(III) ions coordinated to five O(II) ions and 1 phosphate group. Phosphate is very active photosynthetically with sunlight. Most of the phosphate groups that are coordinated to the iron ions lie on the outside of the crystalline structure in ferritin, and are used to bind the mineral to the residues on the inside of the ferritin protein shell. When the Fe(III) in the crystalline mineral is reduced (added electrons from phosphorus or the charge separation of water by sunlight) to Fe(II) state, the iron becomes solvated in blood plasma and ferritin releases the solvated iron to tissues, in this form Fe(H2O)62+, through ferritin's 3-fold polar channel. In order for iron to be released from ferritin, the mineral lattice must be dissolved (i.e., to allow the iron atoms to break away from the lattice structure deep inside ferritin). This removal is accomplished by reducing iron from the Fe(III) (ferric) oxidation state to the Fe(II) (ferrous) oxidation state. In the Fe(II) state, iron breaks away from the lattice as the Fe2+ ion. The POSITIVE CHARGE of the Fe2+ ion attracts the electronegative oxygen atoms (ELECTRONEGATIVE) in water, and so a water "cage" forms around the ion. That cage is a Farady cage which shelters the iron from other electromagnetic waves. Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload. If someone's environment ruins the electrostatic mechanism expalined above for any reason it will effect iron status and ferritin levels in the blood. This mechanism is linked to presence or absence of charge in blood plasma or in phosphorus as a dopant. The EZ potential energy holds the key to what your iron status ultimately will be. Anemia is becoming epidemic in women and children these days for a deep reason: humans have created a low quantum yield world and this alters the charge of blood plasma which directly impacts the release of iron from ferritin.

The three-dimensional structure of ferritin is crucial to understand its quantum function within the body. In fact, the three-dimensional stucture of any molecule is critical in determining a molecule's properties and function optically. Hence, to better understand ferritin's role in the body, we must familiarize our self to different types of molecular representations to study ferritin's three-dimensional stucture. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105608/](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpmc%2Farticles%2FPMC3105608%2F&h=ATPuubupVSAldE74JLl95g5n1c1ReQP7hZw9ItMpLm2TAn-1n6MoxP0ceEyqQ599ZStT7KHkrF94LUjiCDPwbfhcaV92JqakpLLocJD9m73mju8U_6dlbJassjQ_aFVnAaANVmg8-Ui3nMfZoJBq28BK0u2zb1pMLXz_xKZNEdGoXagzCYzVqXhLvG1m7R_pdKRJnHp7nZkagJyafAPproRRpElMBT9VhAHSbcNYihHAH-UxWIilgAQLEnWL9XEPdC38)

### taiyang — 2018-02-14T12:26:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-vs-sun-pplements.20865/post-234721>

Thanks for replying, Penny. "Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload..." This statement is the crux of my confusion. ferritin requires iron, no? if you lack iron, your ferritin will be low. am i correct? in order to boost ferritin, you need to not lose iron or you need to supplement iron. are you saying that i should not supplement with iron when i am anemic due to blood loss from heavy, prolonged periods? do you think being in the sun, grounding, and drinking deuterium depleted water is the only thing i need to rebuild my iron stores to their optimal level?

My working concept (subject to change )

If I want to look at alternate realities, I need enough energy to do it, and it's a controlled paying attention, observing, in a meditative state. I don't see the alternative reality as "real" but a bit like remote viewing an information field of all the _potential_ alternative realities or possible futures/pasts, most of which havent manifested, and may never do so under the laws of physics in our universe. I think it's also not just what light/energy you harvest, it's also about what you leak, and where you leak it from. If I'm leaking, I'm vulnerable to losing control of what I'm doing. It's like the difference between actively looking for mental images in an information field, in full control, through the lens of intention, versus passively seeing mental images. Even that can be ok (like getting an answer to a problem or a new idea, or daydreaming) provided I have the filters that ground it in my current reality and I can recognise it for what it is, and what it isn't.

I don't think it had enough contact with the stomach acid, and blitzing it in a blender could have made it - and any water content - lose all its information, instead of my gut harvesting it. I only eat organic plums straight from the tree when they contain the most light and life force. Crazy alert, they don't have to be perfect - ones that have split and repaired show that they are fighters and have produced damage control biochemicals.... I like that, it makes me smile. A plum sat in a fruit bowl indoors for a couple of days, not so interested, it will have lost more light and life force to the environment. I haven't tried putting them back out in the sunshine before eating them, but grown in the dark mushrooms will make vitamin D if put out in the sunshine, so I think it could be possible to add energy and information back, if the plum hadn't gone past the point of no return ...

That blender news is food for thought. I would not like to think that blitzing food in a blender could make it less tasty to the mitochondria like blitzing food in a microwave.
I hope someone who might have some information on the blender blitzing comes forward and enlightens us. I haven't had a microwave for more than 10 years and I will be happy to trash my blender if the news is bad.

### Cpt.Tired — 2018-11-27T06:13:05+0200

I don't think it had enough contact with the stomach acid, and blitzing it in a blender could have made it - and any water content - lose all its information, instead of my gut harvesting it. Whatever, my gut wasn't having it. I've seen kirlian photos of organic versus non organic etc, but I haven't seen anything on blended foods. I only eat organic plums straight from the tree when they contain the most light and life force. Crazy alert, they don't have to be perfect - ones that have split and repaired show that they are fighters and have produced damage control biochemicals.... I like that, it makes me smile. A plum sat in a fruit bowl indoors for a couple of days, not so interested, it will have lost more light and life force to the environment. I haven't tried putting them back out in the sunshine before eating them, but grown in the dark mushrooms will make vitamin D if put out in the sunshine, so I think it could be possible to add energy and information back, if the plum hadn't gone past the point of no return ...

Do mushrooms make vitamin D while in the dark or when exposed to the sun? I didn't quite understand what you typed. I have been curious about this one!

Thanks for sharing your alternate reality experience by the way..for me it was really bizarre! Never felt anything like that! havent felt it since but I was really going hardcore for a few days there in the spring. I had time off for myself so I was juicing, doing coffee enemas, ionic foot baths, trans cranial direct current stimulation, very deep breathing, chewing my seafood so much that I could absorb it in my mouth, grounding, CT etc. i was obsessed! The big variable was the stimulants and the pot smoking, but it was more than that. I've done that but never felt anything like this! I could swear Jack was talking to me in my head screaming "Right on! you slammed the cell membrane shut!subligual DHA?Why didnt I think of that?!" LOL! Of course my mind was making the whole thing up but it seemed so incredibly real you know? Another interesting thing that I noticed is that other peoples faces looked swollen, like my spacetime was running so much faster that other people became larger. I also felt very strong physically.When the doctor gave me an antipsychotic (which would lower dopamine).the first thing that I noticed was that his face got "less chubby".The whole experience I found it to be absolutely fascinating!! But yeah..my dopamine shot up WAAY too high!

### Sue-UK — 2018-11-27T11:21:45+0200

Apparently I can take commercially grown in the dark mushrooms, and put them outside in the summer sunshine and they will make vitamin D. I don't do it for the vitamin D content, but because it adds a seasonal food signal for a skin-gut match up.

### drezy — 2018-11-27T15:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wow.20867/post-256692>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=256687)
>
> Apparently I can take commercially grown in the dark mushrooms, and put them outside in the summer sunshine and they will make vitamin D. I don't do it for the vitamin D content, but because it adds a seasonal food signal for a skin-gut match up.

The truth is even crazier. Even year old dried and sliced mushrooms will gain in D.

" Surprisingly, even sliced and dried mushrooms—including wild ones picked the year before—will soar in vitamin D when placed outdoors under the sun."
[https://fungi.com/blogs/articles/place-mushrooms-in-sunlight-to-get-your-vitamin-d](https://fungi.com/blogs/articles/place-mushrooms-in-sunlight-to-get-your-vitamin-d)

OR

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3897585/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3897585/)

I'm with you on the (wider than just D) food signaling thoughts.

Free market thins the herd (on both sides of the equation), and it's the only thing that will reverse these problems. It's the not the root cause of the issue by any stretch of the imagination.

Have you ever read "Feynman's Rainbow"?

The author is graduate student at Caltech when Feynman is winding down his life, and there comes a part in the book where one of the fellow graduate students admits to falsifying some data.

Feynman hears about it and asks the author:

"Does it bother you more that he lied, or that nobody was smart enough to catch him in the first place?"
